title.zh: 卷二十七下之上 五行志
title.en: Volume 27d: Treatise on the Five Elements 4
title.raw: 卷二十七下之上 五行志 Volume 27d: Treatise on the Five Elements 4

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「思心之不睿，是謂不聖，厥咎霿，厥罰恆風，厥極凶短折。

translations.literal: The Tradition says: "When thought and mind are not wise, this is called un-sagely; its blame is obscuration, its punishment is constant wind, and its extreme is violent death, shortening, and breakage.
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: "When thought and mind are not wise, this is called un-sagely; its blame is obscuration, its punishment is constant wind, and its extreme is violent death, shortening, and breakage.

sentences.zh: 時則有脂夜之妖，時則有華孽，時則有牛禍，時則有心腹之痾，時則有黃眚黃祥，時則有金木水火沴土。」

translations.literal: Then at times there are eerie nocturnal grease-omens, floral prodigies, ox calamities, afflictions of the heart and abdomen, yellow blights and yellow portents, and cases where metal, wood, water, and fire afflict earth.
translations.idiomatic: Then at times there are eerie nocturnal grease-omens, floral prodigies, ox calamities, afflictions of the heart and abdomen, yellow blights and yellow portents, and cases where metal, wood, water, and fire afflict earth.

sentences.zh: 「思心之不睿，是謂不聖。」

translations.literal: "Thought and mind not wise: this is called un-sagely."
translations.idiomatic: "Thought and mind not wise: this is called un-sagely."

sentences.zh: 思心者，心思慮也；

translations.literal: Thought-mind means the heart's faculty of deliberation.
translations.idiomatic: Thought-mind means the heart's faculty of deliberation.

sentences.zh: 睿，寬也。

translations.literal: Rui means broad-mindedness.
translations.idiomatic: Rui means broad-mindedness.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「居上不寬，吾何以觀之哉！」

translations.literal: Confucius said: "If one above is not broad-minded, by what can I judge him?"
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said: "If one above is not broad-minded, by what can I judge him?"

sentences.zh: 言上不寬大包容臣下，則不能居聖位。

translations.literal: It means that if those above are not broad and tolerant of ministers below, they cannot occupy the sagely position.
translations.idiomatic: It means that if those above are not broad and tolerant of ministers below, they cannot occupy the sagely position.

sentences.zh: 貌言視聽，以心為主，四者皆失，則區霿無識，故其咎霿也。

translations.literal: Bearing, speech, sight, and hearing are led by the mind; when all four fail, there is dim obscurity without understanding, hence its blame is obscuration.
translations.idiomatic: Bearing, speech, sight, and hearing are led by the mind; when all four fail, there is dim obscurity without understanding, hence its blame is obscuration.

sentences.zh: 雨旱寒奧，亦以風為本，四氣皆亂，故其罰常風也。

translations.literal: Rain, drought, cold, and heat all take wind as root; when all four qi are disordered, therefore the punishment is constant wind.
translations.idiomatic: Rain, drought, cold, and heat all take wind as root; when all four qi are disordered, therefore the punishment is constant wind.

sentences.zh: 常風傷物，故其極凶短折也。

translations.literal: Constant wind harms things; therefore its extreme is violent death, shortening, and breakage.
translations.idiomatic: Constant wind harms things; therefore its extreme is violent death, shortening, and breakage.

sentences.zh: 傷人曰凶，禽獸曰短，屮木曰折。

translations.literal: For humans it is violent death, for birds and beasts shortening, for plants and trees breakage.
translations.idiomatic: For humans it is violent death, for birds and beasts shortening, for plants and trees breakage.

sentences.zh: 一曰，凶，夭也；

translations.literal: Another view: violent means untimely death;
translations.idiomatic: Another view: violent means untimely death;

sentences.zh: 兄喪弟曰短，父喪子曰折。

translations.literal: when an elder brother loses a younger it is shortening; when a father loses a son it is breakage.
translations.idiomatic: when an elder brother loses a younger it is shortening; when a father loses a son it is breakage.

sentences.zh: 在人腹中，肥而包裹心者脂也，心區霿則冥晦，故有脂夜之妖。

translations.literal: Within a person's abdomen, the fat that wraps the heart is called zhi; when the heart is clouded, darkness follows, and thus there are omens of greasy night-obscurity.
translations.idiomatic: Within a person's abdomen, the fat that wraps the heart is called zhi; when the heart is clouded, darkness follows, and thus there are omens of greasy night-obscurity.

sentences.zh: 一曰，有脂物而夜為妖，若脂水夜汙人衣，淫之象也。

translations.literal: Another view: if greasy substances at night become anomalous, like greasy water staining clothes at night, it symbolizes licentiousness.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: if greasy substances at night become anomalous, like greasy water staining clothes at night, it symbolizes licentiousness.

sentences.zh: 一曰，夜妖者，雲風並起而杳冥，故與常風同象也。

translations.literal: Another view: night anomalies are when clouds and wind rise together into darkness, therefore sharing the same image as constant wind.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: night anomalies are when clouds and wind rise together into darkness, therefore sharing the same image as constant wind.

sentences.zh: 溫而風則生螟螣，有裸蟲之孽。

translations.literal: Warmth plus wind generates borers and locusts; there are prodigies of naked insects.
translations.idiomatic: Warmth plus wind generates borers and locusts; there are prodigies of naked insects.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為於易巽為風為木，卦在三月四月，繼陽而治，主木之華實。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that in the Changes Xun is wind and wood; this trigram governs the third and fourth months, continuing yang and governing wood's flowers and fruit.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that in the Changes Xun is wind and wood; this trigram governs the third and fourth months, continuing yang and governing wood's flowers and fruit.

sentences.zh: 風氣盛，至秋冬木復華，故有華孽。

translations.literal: When wind qi is excessive, in autumn and winter wood flowers again, therefore there are floral prodigies.
translations.idiomatic: When wind qi is excessive, in autumn and winter wood flowers again, therefore there are floral prodigies.

sentences.zh: 一曰，地氣盛則秋冬復華。

translations.literal: Another view: when earth qi is excessive, flowers bloom again in autumn and winter.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: when earth qi is excessive, flowers bloom again in autumn and winter.

sentences.zh: 一曰，華者色也，土為內事，為女孽也。

translations.literal: Another view: "flower" means color; earth governs inner affairs, thus a female prodigy.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: "flower" means color; earth governs inner affairs, thus a female prodigy.

sentences.zh: 於易坤為土為牛，牛大心而不能思慮，思心氣毀，故有牛禍。

translations.literal: In the Changes Kun is earth and ox. Oxen have large hearts yet cannot deliberate; when thought-mind qi is damaged, therefore there are ox calamities.
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes Kun is earth and ox. Oxen have large hearts yet cannot deliberate; when thought-mind qi is damaged, therefore there are ox calamities.

sentences.zh: 一曰，牛多死及為怪，亦是也。

translations.literal: Another view: many oxen dying or becoming strange also belongs here.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: many oxen dying or becoming strange also belongs here.

sentences.zh: 及人，則多病心腹者，故有心腹之痾。

translations.literal: For people, many have diseases of heart and belly; therefore there are heart-belly afflictions.
translations.idiomatic: For people, many have diseases of heart and belly; therefore there are heart-belly afflictions.

sentences.zh: 土色黃，故有黃眚黃祥。

translations.literal: Earth's color is yellow; therefore there are yellow blights and yellow portents.
translations.idiomatic: Earth's color is yellow; therefore there are yellow blights and yellow portents.

sentences.zh: 凡思心傷者病土氣，土氣病則金木水火沴之，故曰「時則有金木水火沴土」。

translations.literal: All damage to thought-mind is illness of earth qi; when earth qi is ill, metal, wood, water, and fire afflict it. Therefore it says "then at times there are metal, wood, water, and fire afflicting earth."
translations.idiomatic: All damage to thought-mind is illness of earth qi; when earth qi is ill, metal, wood, water, and fire afflict it. Therefore it says "then at times there are metal, wood, water, and fire afflicting earth."

sentences.zh: 不言「惟」而獨曰「時則有」者，非一衝氣所沴，明其異大也。

translations.literal: It does not say "only" but specifically says "then at times" because this is not one conflicting qi alone; it shows the anomaly is greater.
translations.idiomatic: It does not say "only" but specifically says "then at times" because this is not one conflicting qi alone; it shows the anomaly is greater.

sentences.zh: 其極曰凶短折，順之，其福曰考終命。

translations.literal: Its extreme is violent death, shortening, and breakage. Following the proper course, its blessing is to complete one's allotted span.
translations.idiomatic: Its extreme is violent death, shortening, and breakage. Following the proper course, its blessing is to complete one's allotted span.

sentences.zh: 劉歆思心傳曰時則有臝蟲之孽，謂螟螣之屬也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin's transmission on thought-mind says there are prodigies of naked insects, namely borers and locust kinds.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin's transmission on thought-mind says there are prodigies of naked insects, namely borers and locust kinds.

sentences.zh: 庶徵之常風，劉向以為春秋無其應。

translations.literal: For the common sign of constant wind, Liu Xiang held there is no corresponding case in Spring and Autumn.
translations.idiomatic: For the common sign of constant wind, Liu Xiang held there is no corresponding case in Spring and Autumn.

sentences.zh: 釐公十六年「正月，六鶂退蜚，過宋都」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi year sixteen: "In first month, six yi birds turned back in flight, passing over the Song capital."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year sixteen: "In first month, six yi birds turned back in flight, passing over the Song capital."

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰「風也」。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says: "It was wind."
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says: "It was wind."

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為風發於它所，至宋而高，鶂高蜚而逢之，則退。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that wind arose elsewhere and grew strong at Song; the yi birds flew high and met it, then were driven back.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that wind arose elsewhere and grew strong at Song; the yi birds flew high and met it, then were driven back.

sentences.zh: 經以見者為文，故記退蜚；

translations.literal: The Classic records what was seen, therefore it records their retreating flight;
translations.idiomatic: The Classic records what was seen, therefore it records their retreating flight;

sentences.zh: 傳以實應著，言風，常風之罰也。

translations.literal: the Tradition sets out the real correspondence, saying it was wind, punishment of constant wind.
translations.idiomatic: the Tradition sets out the real correspondence, saying it was wind, punishment of constant wind.

sentences.zh: 象宋襄公區霿自用，不容臣下，逆司馬子魚之諫，而與彊楚爭盟，後六年為楚所執，應六鶂之數云。

translations.literal: It symbolized Duke Xiang of Song's obscured self-will, not tolerating ministers below, rejecting Marshal Ziyu's remonstrance, and competing with strong Chu for alliance. Six years later he was captured by Chu, corresponding to the number six of the yi birds.
translations.idiomatic: It symbolized Duke Xiang of Song's obscured self-will, not tolerating ministers below, rejecting Marshal Ziyu's remonstrance, and competing with strong Chu for alliance. Six years later he was captured by Chu, corresponding to the number six of the yi birds.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「潛龍勿用，眾逆同志，至德乃潛，厥異風。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Hidden dragon: do not act. When the masses rebel in one mind and highest virtue is submerged, the anomaly is wind.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Hidden dragon: do not act. When the masses rebel in one mind and highest virtue is submerged, the anomaly is wind.

sentences.zh: 其風也，行不解物，不長，雨小而傷。

translations.literal: Such wind does not nourish things or foster growth; rainfall is slight yet still harmful.
translations.idiomatic: Such wind does not nourish things or foster growth; rainfall is slight yet still harmful.

sentences.zh: 政悖德隱茲謂亂，厥風先風不雨，大風暴起，發屋折木。

translations.literal: When governance is perverse and virtue concealed, this is called disorder: first wind without rain, then great wind suddenly rising, tearing roofs and breaking trees.
translations.idiomatic: When governance is perverse and virtue concealed, this is called disorder: first wind without rain, then great wind suddenly rising, tearing roofs and breaking trees.

sentences.zh: 守義不進茲謂耄，厥風與雲俱起，折五穀莖。

translations.literal: When one guards righteousness yet does not advance, this is called senility: its wind rises with clouds, snapping stalks of the five grains.
translations.idiomatic: When one guards righteousness yet does not advance, this is called senility: its wind rises with clouds, snapping stalks of the five grains.

sentences.zh: 臣易上政，茲謂不順，厥風大焱發屋。

translations.literal: When ministers alter the ruler's government, this is called noncompliance: its wind is blazing and tears roofs.
translations.idiomatic: When ministers alter the ruler's government, this is called noncompliance: its wind is blazing and tears roofs.

sentences.zh: 賦斂不理茲謂禍，厥風絕經紀，止即溫，溫即蟲。

translations.literal: When taxation and levies are mismanaged, this is called calamity: its wind cuts off order; when it stops there is warmth, and warmth brings insects.
translations.idiomatic: When taxation and levies are mismanaged, this is called calamity: its wind cuts off order; when it stops there is warmth, and warmth brings insects.

sentences.zh: 侯專封茲謂不統，厥風疾，而樹不搖，穀不成。

translations.literal: When feudal lords control enfeoffments, this is called lack of unity: its wind is swift yet trees do not sway and grain does not ripen.
translations.idiomatic: When feudal lords control enfeoffments, this is called lack of unity: its wind is swift yet trees do not sway and grain does not ripen.

sentences.zh: 辟不思道利，茲謂無澤，厥風不搖木，旱無雲，傷禾。

translations.literal: When rulers do not think of the way of benefit, this is called lack of grace: its wind does not move trees, drought has no clouds, and crops are harmed.
translations.idiomatic: When rulers do not think of the way of benefit, this is called lack of grace: its wind does not move trees, drought has no clouds, and crops are harmed.

sentences.zh: 公常於利茲謂亂，厥風微而溫，生蟲蝗，害五穀。

translations.literal: When the duke constantly seeks profit, this is called disorder: its wind is slight and warm, breeding locusts and insects that harm the five grains.
translations.idiomatic: When the duke constantly seeks profit, this is called disorder: its wind is slight and warm, breeding locusts and insects that harm the five grains.

sentences.zh: 棄正作淫茲謂惑，厥風溫，螟蟲起，害有益人之物。

translations.literal: Abandoning the proper and practicing licentiousness is called delusion: its wind is warm, borers arise, and useful things to people are harmed.
translations.idiomatic: Abandoning the proper and practicing licentiousness is called delusion: its wind is warm, borers arise, and useful things to people are harmed.

sentences.zh: 侯不朝茲謂叛，厥風無恆，地變赤而殺人。」

translations.literal: When feudal lords do not come to court, this is called rebellion: its wind is erratic, the earth turns red, and people die."
translations.idiomatic: When feudal lords do not come to court, this is called rebellion: its wind is erratic, the earth turns red, and people die."

sentences.zh: 文帝二年六月，淮南王都壽春大風毀民室，殺人。

translations.literal: In sixth month of Emperor Wen year two, in Shouchun, capital of the Huainan king, great wind destroyed commoners' houses and killed people.
translations.idiomatic: In sixth month of Emperor Wen year two, in Shouchun, capital of the Huainan king, great wind destroyed commoners' houses and killed people.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為是歲南越反，攻淮南邊，淮南王長破之，後年入朝，殺漢故丞相辟陽侯，上赦之，歸聚姦人謀逆亂，自稱東帝，見異不寤，後遷于蜀，道死廱。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that that year Nanyue rebelled and attacked Huainan's border; King Chang of Huainan broke them. The next year he came to court, killed former Han chancellor Marquis of Biyang, was pardoned by the throne, then returned and gathered evil men to plot rebellion, calling himself Eastern Emperor. Seeing anomalies he did not awaken; later he was moved to Shu and died on the road in Yong.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that that year Nanyue rebelled and attacked Huainan's border; King Chang of Huainan broke them. The next year he came to court, killed former Han chancellor Marquis of Biyang, was pardoned by the throne, then returned and gathered evil men to plot rebellion, calling himself Eastern Emperor. Seeing anomalies he did not awaken; later he was moved to Shu and died on the road in Yong.

sentences.zh: 文帝五年，吳暴風雨，壞城官府民室。

translations.literal: In Emperor Wen year five, in Wu there were violent winds and rains, damaging city walls, offices, and homes.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Wen year five, in Wu there were violent winds and rains, damaging city walls, offices, and homes.

sentences.zh: 時吳王濞謀為逆亂，天戒數見，終不改寤，後卒誅滅。

translations.literal: At that time King Pi of Wu plotted rebellion. Heaven's warnings appeared repeatedly, yet he never reformed, and in the end he was executed and destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: At that time King Pi of Wu plotted rebellion. Heaven's warnings appeared repeatedly, yet he never reformed, and in the end he was executed and destroyed.

sentences.zh: 五年十月，楚王都彭城大風從東南來，毀巿門，殺人。

translations.literal: In tenth month of year five, in Pengcheng, capital of the King of Chu, a great wind came from the southeast, destroyed market gates, and killed people.
translations.idiomatic: In tenth month of year five, in Pengcheng, capital of the King of Chu, a great wind came from the southeast, destroyed market gates, and killed people.

sentences.zh: 是月王戊初嗣立，後坐淫削國，與吳王謀反，刑僇諫者。

translations.literal: That month King Wu had just inherited the throne; later, convicted of licentiousness, his state was reduced. He plotted rebellion with the King of Wu and executed remonstrants.
translations.idiomatic: That month King Wu had just inherited the throne; later, convicted of licentiousness, his state was reduced. He plotted rebellion with the King of Wu and executed remonstrants.

sentences.zh: 吳在楚東南，天戒若曰，勿與吳為惡，將敗巿朝。

translations.literal: Wu lay southeast of Chu. Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not do evil with Wu, or your markets and court will be ruined.
translations.idiomatic: Wu lay southeast of Chu. Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not do evil with Wu, or your markets and court will be ruined.

sentences.zh: 王戊不寤，卒隨吳亡。

translations.literal: King Wu did not awaken and eventually perished with Wu.
translations.idiomatic: King Wu did not awaken and eventually perished with Wu.

sentences.zh: 釐公十五年「九月己卯晦，震夷伯之廟」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi year fifteen: "On the last day, jimao, of the ninth month, thunder struck the temple of Yi Bo."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year fifteen: "On the last day, jimao, of the ninth month, thunder struck the temple of Yi Bo."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為晦，暝也；

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that "last day" means darkness;
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that "last day" means darkness;

sentences.zh: 震，雷也。

translations.literal: "zhen" means thunder.
translations.idiomatic: "zhen" means thunder.

sentences.zh: 夷伯，世大夫，正書雷，其廟獨冥。

translations.literal: Yi Bo was a hereditary grandee; the record properly writes thunder, and his temple alone was darkened.
translations.idiomatic: Yi Bo was a hereditary grandee; the record properly writes thunder, and his temple alone was darkened.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，勿使大夫世官，將專事暝晦。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not let grandees hold hereditary office, or they will monopolize affairs in darkness.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not let grandees hold hereditary office, or they will monopolize affairs in darkness.

sentences.zh: 明年，公子季友卒，果世官，政在季氏。

translations.literal: The next year Prince Jiyou died; hereditary office indeed continued, and power rested with the Ji clan.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Prince Jiyou died; hereditary office indeed continued, and power rested with the Ji clan.

sentences.zh: 至成公十六年「六月甲午晦」，正晝皆暝，陰為陽，臣制君也。

translations.literal: By Duke Cheng year sixteen, "on jiawu day at month's end in sixth month," broad daylight became dark: yin as yang, ministers controlling the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: By Duke Cheng year sixteen, "on jiawu day at month's end in sixth month," broad daylight became dark: yin as yang, ministers controlling the ruler.

sentences.zh: 成公不寤，其冬季氏殺公子偃。

translations.literal: Duke Cheng did not awaken; that winter the Ji clan killed Prince Yan.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Cheng did not awaken; that winter the Ji clan killed Prince Yan.

sentences.zh: 季氏萌於釐公，大於成公，此其應也。

translations.literal: The Ji clan began under Duke Xi and grew great under Duke Cheng; this was its response.
translations.idiomatic: The Ji clan began under Duke Xi and grew great under Duke Cheng; this was its response.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒以為夷伯，季氏之孚也，陪臣不當有廟。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu held Yi Bo was a dependent of the Ji clan; dependent ministers should not have their own temple.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu held Yi Bo was a dependent of the Ji clan; dependent ministers should not have their own temple.

sentences.zh: 震者雷也，晦暝，雷擊其廟，明當絕去僭差之類也。

translations.literal: Thunder striking his temple in dark month-end showed that usurping, deviant kinds should be cut off.
translations.idiomatic: Thunder striking his temple in dark month-end showed that usurping, deviant kinds should be cut off.

sentences.zh: 向又以為此皆所謂夜妖者也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang further held these were all so-called night anomalies.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang further held these were all so-called night anomalies.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為春秋及朔言朔，及晦言晦，人道所不及，則天震之。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that Spring and Autumn names the new moon as new moon and month-end as month-end; where human governance cannot reach, Heaven thunders it.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that Spring and Autumn names the new moon as new moon and month-end as month-end; where human governance cannot reach, Heaven thunders it.

sentences.zh: 展氏有隱慝，故天加誅於其祖夷伯之廟以譴告之也。

translations.literal: The Zhan clan had hidden wrongdoing, so Heaven punished their ancestor Yi Bo's temple to admonish them.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhan clan had hidden wrongdoing, so Heaven punished their ancestor Yi Bo's temple to admonish them.

sentences.zh: 成公十六年「六月甲午晦，晉侯及楚子、鄭伯戰于鄢陵」。

translations.literal: In Duke Cheng year sixteen: "On jiawu day at month's end in sixth month, the marquis of Jin and the king of Chu and earl of Zheng fought at Yanling."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Cheng year sixteen: "On jiawu day at month's end in sixth month, the marquis of Jin and the king of Chu and earl of Zheng fought at Yanling."

sentences.zh: 皆月晦云。

translations.literal: All happened at month's end.
translations.idiomatic: All happened at month's end.

sentences.zh: 隱公五年「秋，螟」。

translations.literal: In Duke Yin year five: "In autumn, borers."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Yin year five: "In autumn, borers."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為時公觀漁于棠，貪利之應也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that the duke then watched fishing at Tang; this was a response to greed for profit.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that the duke then watched fishing at Tang; this was a response to greed for profit.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為又逆臧釐伯之諫，貪利區瓒，以生臝蟲之孽也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held that he also rejected Zang Xibo's remonstrance and greedily coveted trifles, producing a prodigy of naked insects.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held that he also rejected Zang Xibo's remonstrance and greedily coveted trifles, producing a prodigy of naked insects.

sentences.zh: 八年「九月，螟」。

translations.literal: In year eight: "In the ninth month, borers."
translations.idiomatic: In year eight: "In the ninth month, borers."

sentences.zh: 時鄭伯以邴將易許田，有貪利心。

translations.literal: At that time the earl of Zheng sought to exchange Xu fields with Bing, showing greedy intent.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the earl of Zheng sought to exchange Xu fields with Bing, showing greedy intent.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「臣安祿茲謂貪，厥災蟲，蟲食根。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ministers rest secure in salary, this is called greed; its disaster is insects, and insects eat roots.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ministers rest secure in salary, this is called greed; its disaster is insects, and insects eat roots.

sentences.zh: 德無常茲謂煩，蟲食葉。

translations.literal: When virtue lacks constancy, this is called vexation; insects eat leaves.
translations.idiomatic: When virtue lacks constancy, this is called vexation; insects eat leaves.

sentences.zh: 不絀無德，蟲食本。

translations.literal: Not dismissing the unvirtuous, insects eat the trunk.
translations.idiomatic: Not dismissing the unvirtuous, insects eat the trunk.

sentences.zh: 與東作爭，茲謂不時，蟲食節。

translations.literal: Competing with spring tilling is called untimely; insects eat nodes.
translations.idiomatic: Competing with spring tilling is called untimely; insects eat nodes.

sentences.zh: 蔽惡生孽，蟲食心。」

translations.literal: Concealing evil breeds prodigies; insects eat the heart."
translations.idiomatic: Concealing evil breeds prodigies; insects eat the heart."

sentences.zh: 嚴公六年「秋，螟」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhuang year six: "In autumn, borers."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhuang year six: "In autumn, borers."

sentences.zh: 董仲舒、劉向以為先是衛侯朔出奔齊，齊侯會諸侯納朔，許諸侯賂。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Shuo of Wei fled to Qi; the marquis of Qi convened feudal lords to restore Shuo and accepted bribes from them.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu and Liu Xiang held that before this Duke Shuo of Wei fled to Qi; the marquis of Qi convened feudal lords to restore Shuo and accepted bribes from them.

sentences.zh: 齊人歸衛寶，魯受之，貪利應也。

translations.literal: Qi returned Wei treasures and Lu accepted them, a response to greed for profit.
translations.idiomatic: Qi returned Wei treasures and Lu accepted them, a response to greed for profit.

sentences.zh: 文帝後六年秋，螟。

translations.literal: In autumn of Emperor Wen's later year six, borers appeared.
translations.idiomatic: In autumn of Emperor Wen's later year six, borers appeared.

sentences.zh: 是歲匈奴大入上郡、雲中，烽火通長安，遣三將軍屯邊，三將軍屯京師。

translations.literal: That year the Xiongnu greatly invaded Shang and Yunzhong; beacon fires reached Chang'an, and three generals were sent to garrison the frontier and three to garrison the capital.
translations.idiomatic: That year the Xiongnu greatly invaded Shang and Yunzhong; beacon fires reached Chang'an, and three generals were sent to garrison the frontier and three to garrison the capital.

sentences.zh: 宣公三年，「郊牛之口傷，改卜牛，牛死」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xuan year three: "The mouth of the suburban sacrificial ox was injured; divination was redone for another ox, and that ox died."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xuan year three: "The mouth of the suburban sacrificial ox was injured; divination was redone for another ox, and that ox died."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近牛禍也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held this was a near ox calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held this was a near ox calamity.

sentences.zh: 是時宣公與公子遂謀共殺子赤而立，又以喪娶，區霿昏亂。

translations.literal: At that time Duke Xuan and Prince Sui plotted to kill Zichi and enthrone Xuan, and moreover married during mourning, obscured and chaotic.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Duke Xuan and Prince Sui plotted to kill Zichi and enthrone Xuan, and moreover married during mourning, obscured and chaotic.

sentences.zh: 亂成於口，幸有季文子得免於禍，天猶惡之，生則不饗其祀，死則災燔其廟。

translations.literal: Disorder was completed through the mouth. Luckily Jisun Wenzi escaped disaster; Heaven still hated it: alive, it did not accept his sacrifices; dead, disaster burned his temple.
translations.idiomatic: Disorder was completed through the mouth. Luckily Jisun Wenzi escaped disaster; Heaven still hated it: alive, it did not accept his sacrifices; dead, disaster burned his temple.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒指略同。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu's main point was roughly the same.

sentences.zh: 秦孝文王五年，斿朐衍，有獻五足牛者。

translations.literal: In year five of King Xiaowen of Qin, at Youquyan, someone presented a five-legged ox.
translations.idiomatic: In year five of King Xiaowen of Qin, at Youquyan, someone presented a five-legged ox.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近牛禍也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held this was a near ox calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held this was a near ox calamity.

sentences.zh: 先是文惠王初都咸陽，廣大宮室，南臨渭，北臨涇，思心失，逆土氣。

translations.literal: Before this, King Wenhui had first made Xianyang the capital, enlarging palaces, facing the Wei in the south and the Jing in the north; thought-mind failed and went against earth qi.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, King Wenhui had first made Xianyang the capital, enlarging palaces, facing the Wei in the south and the Jing in the north; thought-mind failed and went against earth qi.

sentences.zh: 足者止也，戒秦建止奢泰，將致危亡。

translations.literal: Feet signify stopping; it warned Qin to halt extravagant grandeur, or it would bring danger and ruin.
translations.idiomatic: Feet signify stopping; it warned Qin to halt extravagant grandeur, or it would bring danger and ruin.

sentences.zh: 秦遂不改，至於離宮三百，復起阿房，未成而亡。

translations.literal: Qin never changed: by the time of three hundred detached palaces and then Afang raised again, it perished before completion.
translations.idiomatic: Qin never changed: by the time of three hundred detached palaces and then Afang raised again, it perished before completion.

sentences.zh: 一曰，牛以力為人用，足所以行也。

translations.literal: Another view: oxen serve people by strength, and feet are for movement.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: oxen serve people by strength, and feet are for movement.

sentences.zh: 其後秦大用民力轉輸，起負海至北邊，天下叛之。

translations.literal: Later Qin greatly exploited people's labor in transport, moving burdens from the sea to the northern frontier, and all under heaven rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: Later Qin greatly exploited people's labor in transport, moving burdens from the sea to the northern frontier, and all under heaven rebelled.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「興繇役，奪民時，厥妖牛生五足。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When corvée is raised and people's seasons are seized, its portent is an ox born with five legs."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When corvée is raised and people's seasons are seized, its portent is an ox born with five legs."

sentences.zh: 景帝中六年，梁孝王田北山，有獻牛，足上出背上。

translations.literal: In Emperor Jing's middle year six, while King Xiao of Liang hunted in the northern mountains, someone presented an ox whose foot emerged on its back.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Jing's middle year six, while King Xiao of Liang hunted in the northern mountains, someone presented an ox whose foot emerged on its back.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近牛禍。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near ox calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near ox calamity.

sentences.zh: 先是孝王驕奢，起苑方三百里，宮館閣道相連三十餘里。

translations.literal: Before this, King Xiao was arrogant and extravagant, building parks three hundred li square, with palaces, halls, and elevated passages linked for over thirty li.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, King Xiao was arrogant and extravagant, building parks three hundred li square, with palaces, halls, and elevated passages linked for over thirty li.

sentences.zh: 納於邪臣羊勝之計，欲求為漢嗣，刺殺議臣爰盎，事發，負斧歸死。

translations.literal: He accepted the schemes of the wicked minister Yang Sheng, sought to become Han heir, and had deliberative minister Yuan Ang assassinated. When the matter was exposed, he returned bearing an axe and submitted to death.
translations.idiomatic: He accepted the schemes of the wicked minister Yang Sheng, sought to become Han heir, and had deliberative minister Yuan Ang assassinated. When the matter was exposed, he returned bearing an axe and submitted to death.

sentences.zh: 既退歸國，猶有恨心，內則思慮霿亂，外則土功過制，故牛禍作。

translations.literal: After withdrawing to his state, he still harbored resentment: inwardly his thoughts were obscured and chaotic, outwardly his earthworks exceeded proper limits; therefore an ox calamity arose.
translations.idiomatic: After withdrawing to his state, he still harbored resentment: inwardly his thoughts were obscured and chaotic, outwardly his earthworks exceeded proper limits; therefore an ox calamity arose.

sentences.zh: 足而出於背，下奸上之象也。

translations.literal: A foot emerging from the back is the image of lower treachery against above.
translations.idiomatic: A foot emerging from the back is the image of lower treachery against above.

sentences.zh: 猶不能自解，發疾暴死，又凶短之極也。

translations.literal: He still could not settle himself, then suddenly fell ill and died violently, another extreme case of lethal truncation.
translations.idiomatic: He still could not settle himself, then suddenly fell ill and died violently, another extreme case of lethal truncation.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳昭公二十一年春，周景王將鑄無挛鍾，叠州鳩曰：「王其以心疾死乎！

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says: in spring of Duke Zhao year twenty-one, King Jing of Zhou was about to cast the Wuluan bells. Dan Zhoujiu said: "Will the king die of heart illness?
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says: in spring of Duke Zhao year twenty-one, King Jing of Zhou was about to cast the Wuluan bells. Dan Zhoujiu said: "Will the king die of heart illness?

sentences.zh: 夫天子省風以作樂，小者不窕，大者不摦。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven examines winds to make music: small bells must not be too narrow, large bells must not be too broad.
translations.idiomatic: The Son of Heaven examines winds to make music: small bells must not be too narrow, large bells must not be too broad.

sentences.zh: 摦則不容，心是以感，感實生疾。

translations.literal: If broad, they do not harmonize; the heart is affected by this, and feeling truly gives rise to illness.
translations.idiomatic: If broad, they do not harmonize; the heart is affected by this, and feeling truly gives rise to illness.

sentences.zh: 今鍾摦矣，王心弗龊，其能久乎？」

translations.literal: Now the bells are broad; the king's heart is not constrained. How can he last long?"
translations.idiomatic: Now the bells are broad; the king's heart is not constrained. How can he last long?"

sentences.zh: 劉向以為是時景王好聽淫聲，適庶不明，思心霿亂，明年以心疾崩，近心腹之痾，凶短之極者也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that at this time King Jing delighted in licentious sounds, failed to distinguish legitimate and secondary lines, and his thought-mind was obscured and chaotic; the next year he died of heart illness, a near affliction of heart and belly and extreme of violent shortening.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that at this time King Jing delighted in licentious sounds, failed to distinguish legitimate and secondary lines, and his thought-mind was obscured and chaotic; the next year he died of heart illness, a near affliction of heart and belly and extreme of violent shortening.

sentences.zh: 昭二十五年春，魯叔孫昭子聘于宋，元公與燕，飲酒樂，語相泣也。

translations.literal: In spring of Zhao year twenty-five, Shusun Zhaozi of Lu went on mission to Song. Duke Yuan hosted a banquet where, amid drinking and music, their talk turned to weeping.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of Zhao year twenty-five, Shusun Zhaozi of Lu went on mission to Song. Duke Yuan hosted a banquet where, amid drinking and music, their talk turned to weeping.

sentences.zh: 樂祈佐，告人曰：「今茲君與叔孫其皆死乎！

translations.literal: Yue Qi Zuo told others: "Will this ruler and Shusun both die this year?
translations.idiomatic: Yue Qi Zuo told others: "Will this ruler and Shusun both die this year?

sentences.zh: 吾聞之，哀樂而樂哀，皆喪心也。

translations.literal: I have heard that to mourn in joy and rejoice in mourning are both losses of heart.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that to mourn in joy and rejoice in mourning are both losses of heart.

sentences.zh: 心之精爽，是謂魂魄；

translations.literal: The refined essence of the heart is what is called hun and po;
translations.idiomatic: The refined essence of the heart is what is called hun and po;

sentences.zh: 魂魄去之，何以能久？」

translations.literal: If hun and po depart, how can one endure long?"
translations.idiomatic: If hun and po depart, how can one endure long?"

sentences.zh: 冬十月，叔孫昭子死；

translations.literal: In winter, tenth month, Shusun Zhaozi died;
translations.idiomatic: In winter, tenth month, Shusun Zhaozi died;

sentences.zh: 十一月，宋元公卒。

translations.literal: in eleventh month, Duke Yuan of Song died.
translations.idiomatic: in eleventh month, Duke Yuan of Song died.

sentences.zh: 成帝建始元年四月辛丑夜，西北有如火光。

translations.literal: On xinchou night of fourth month, Jianshi year one under Emperor Cheng, in the northwest there was a glow like fire.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou night of fourth month, Jianshi year one under Emperor Cheng, in the northwest there was a glow like fire.

sentences.zh: 壬寅晨，大風從西北起，雲氣赤黃，四塞天下，終日夜下著地者黃土塵也。

translations.literal: At dawn on renyin, a great wind rose from the northwest; cloud qi was red-yellow, filling the whole sky, and through day and night what fell to the ground was yellow dust.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn on renyin, a great wind rose from the northwest; cloud qi was red-yellow, filling the whole sky, and through day and night what fell to the ground was yellow dust.

sentences.zh: 是歲，帝元舅大司馬大將軍王鳳始用事；

translations.literal: That year the emperor's maternal uncle, Grand Marshal and Grand General Wang Feng, first came to power;
translations.idiomatic: That year the emperor's maternal uncle, Grand Marshal and Grand General Wang Feng, first came to power;

sentences.zh: 又封鳳母弟崇為安成侯，食邑萬戶；

translations.literal: he also enfeoffed Feng's younger full brother Chong as Marquis of Ancheng with ten thousand households;
translations.idiomatic: he also enfeoffed Feng's younger full brother Chong as Marquis of Ancheng with ten thousand households;

sentences.zh: 庶弟譚等五人賜爵關內侯，食邑三千戶。

translations.literal: and granted rank of Marquis Within the Passes to five younger half-brothers including Tan, each with three thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: and granted rank of Marquis Within the Passes to five younger half-brothers including Tan, each with three thousand households.

sentences.zh: 復益封鳳五千戶，悉封譚等為列侯，是為五侯。

translations.literal: Then Feng's fief was increased by five thousand households and all Tan and others were enfeoffed as full marquises; these were the Five Marquises.
translations.idiomatic: Then Feng's fief was increased by five thousand households and all Tan and others were enfeoffed as full marquises; these were the Five Marquises.

sentences.zh: 哀帝即位，封外屬丁氏、傅氏、周氏、鄭氏凡六人為列侯。

translations.literal: When Emperor Ai took the throne, he enfeoffed six maternal-line relatives of the Ding, Fu, Zhou, and Zheng clans as marquises.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Ai took the throne, he enfeoffed six maternal-line relatives of the Ding, Fu, Zhou, and Zheng clans as marquises.

sentences.zh: 楊宣對曰：「五侯封日，天氣赤黃，丁、傅復然。

translations.literal: Yang Xuan replied: "On the day the Five Marquises were enfeoffed, the weather was red-yellow; with Ding and Fu it happened again.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xuan replied: "On the day the Five Marquises were enfeoffed, the weather was red-yellow; with Ding and Fu it happened again.

sentences.zh: 此殆爵土過制，傷亂土氣之祥也。」

translations.literal: This likely signifies ranks and lands exceeding regulation and thereby harming and disordering earth qi."
translations.idiomatic: This likely signifies ranks and lands exceeding regulation and thereby harming and disordering earth qi."

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「經稱『

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "The Classic says,
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "The Classic says,

sentences.zh: 觀其生』，言大臣之義，當觀賢人，知其性行，推而貢之，否則為聞善不與，茲謂不知，厥異黃，厥咎聾，厥災不嗣。

translations.literal: "Observe their growth." It speaks of a great minister's duty: one should observe worthy men, know their character and conduct, and recommend them. Otherwise this is hearing of good and not giving place, called ignorance; its anomaly is yellow, its blame is deafness, its disaster is no heirs.
translations.idiomatic: "Observe their growth." It speaks of a great minister's duty: one should observe worthy men, know their character and conduct, and recommend them. Otherwise this is hearing of good and not giving place, called ignorance; its anomaly is yellow, its blame is deafness, its disaster is no heirs.

sentences.zh: 黃者，日上黃光不散如火然，有黃濁氣四塞天下。

translations.literal: Yellow means yellow light above the sun not dispersing like fire, with turbid yellow qi filling the world.
translations.idiomatic: Yellow means yellow light above the sun not dispersing like fire, with turbid yellow qi filling the world.

sentences.zh: 蔽賢絕道，故災異至絕世也。

translations.literal: Blocking worthies and cutting off the Way, therefore the disaster-anomaly reaches extinction of the line.
translations.idiomatic: Blocking worthies and cutting off the Way, therefore the disaster-anomaly reaches extinction of the line.

sentences.zh: 經曰『良馬逐』。

translations.literal: The Classic says, "Good horses are driven forward."
translations.idiomatic: The Classic says, "Good horses are driven forward."

sentences.zh: 逐，進也，言大臣得賢者謀，當顯進其人，否則為下相攘善，茲謂盜明，厥咎亦不嗣，至於身僇家絕。」

translations.literal: Drive means advance. It says when a great minister obtains worthy counsel, he should prominently advance that person; otherwise those below seize away the good, called stealing brightness, and its blame is likewise no heirs, reaching personal execution and family extinction."
translations.idiomatic: Drive means advance. It says when a great minister obtains worthy counsel, he should prominently advance that person; otherwise those below seize away the good, called stealing brightness, and its blame is likewise no heirs, reaching personal execution and family extinction."

sentences.zh: 史記周幽王二年，周三川皆震。

translations.literal: The Records says: in year two of King You of Zhou, all three rivers of Zhou shook.
translations.idiomatic: The Records says: in year two of King You of Zhou, all three rivers of Zhou shook.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為金木水火沴土者也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held this was metal, wood, water, and fire afflicting earth.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held this was metal, wood, water, and fire afflicting earth.

sentences.zh: 伯陽甫曰：「周將亡矣！

translations.literal: Boyang Fu said: "Zhou is about to perish!
translations.idiomatic: Boyang Fu said: "Zhou is about to perish!

sentences.zh: 天地之氣不過其序；

translations.literal: The qi of Heaven and Earth does not exceed its order;
translations.idiomatic: The qi of Heaven and Earth does not exceed its order;

sentences.zh: 若過其序，民亂之也。

translations.literal: if it exceeds order, the people disorder it.
translations.idiomatic: if it exceeds order, the people disorder it.

sentences.zh: 陽伏而不能出，陰迫而不能升，於是有地震。

translations.literal: When yang is submerged and cannot emerge, and yin is pressed and cannot rise, then there are earthquakes.
translations.idiomatic: When yang is submerged and cannot emerge, and yin is pressed and cannot rise, then there are earthquakes.

sentences.zh: 今三川實震，是陽失其所而填陰也。

translations.literal: Now the three rivers truly shook: this means yang lost its place and filled yin.
translations.idiomatic: Now the three rivers truly shook: this means yang lost its place and filled yin.

sentences.zh: 陽失而在陰，原必塞；

translations.literal: If yang is lost and lies in yin, the springs must be blocked;
translations.idiomatic: If yang is lost and lies in yin, the springs must be blocked;

sentences.zh: 原塞，國必亡。

translations.literal: if springs are blocked, the state must perish.
translations.idiomatic: if springs are blocked, the state must perish.

sentences.zh: 夫水，土演而民用也；

translations.literal: Water is what earth channels, and people use it;
translations.idiomatic: Water is what earth channels, and people use it;

sentences.zh: 土無所演，而民乏財用，不亡何待？

translations.literal: if earth has nothing to channel, the people lack resources. If not perish then what awaits?
translations.idiomatic: if earth has nothing to channel, the people lack resources. If not perish then what awaits?

sentences.zh: 昔伊雒竭而夏亡，河竭而商亡，今周德如二代之季，其原又塞，塞必竭；

translations.literal: Formerly when Yi and Luo dried up, Xia perished; when the Yellow dried up, Shang perished. Now Zhou's virtue is like the late generations of those two dynasties, and its springs are also blocked; blockage must lead to drying up.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly when Yi and Luo dried up, Xia perished; when the Yellow dried up, Shang perished. Now Zhou's virtue is like the late generations of those two dynasties, and its springs are also blocked; blockage must lead to drying up.

sentences.zh: 川竭，山必崩。

translations.literal: If rivers dry, mountains must collapse.
translations.idiomatic: If rivers dry, mountains must collapse.

sentences.zh: 夫國必依山川，山崩川竭，亡之徵也。

translations.literal: A state must rely on mountains and rivers; mountain collapse and river exhaustion are signs of ruin.
translations.idiomatic: A state must rely on mountains and rivers; mountain collapse and river exhaustion are signs of ruin.

sentences.zh: 若國亡，不過十年，數之紀也。」

translations.literal: If the state perishes, it will be within ten years; this is the cycle of numbers."
translations.idiomatic: If the state perishes, it will be within ten years; this is the cycle of numbers."

sentences.zh: 是歲二川竭，岐山崩。

translations.literal: That year two rivers dried up and Mount Qi collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: That year two rivers dried up and Mount Qi collapsed.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為陽失在陰者，謂火氣來煎枯水，故川竭也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that yang losing its place within yin means fire-qi comes to scorch water dry, which is why the rivers were exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that yang losing its place within yin means fire-qi comes to scorch water dry, which is why the rivers were exhausted.

sentences.zh: 山川連體，下竭上崩，事勢然也。

translations.literal: Mountains and rivers are linked as one body: when below dries up, above collapses. Such is the force of things.
translations.idiomatic: Mountains and rivers are linked as one body: when below dries up, above collapses. Such is the force of things.

sentences.zh: 時幽王暴虐，妄誅伐，不聽諫，迷於褒姒，廢其正后，廢后之父申侯與犬戎共攻殺幽王。

translations.literal: At that time King You was violent and abusive, punished and attacked recklessly, would not heed remonstrance, was deluded by Bao Si, deposed his rightful queen; the deposed queen's father, Marquis of Shen, joined Quanrong to attack and kill King You.
translations.idiomatic: At that time King You was violent and abusive, punished and attacked recklessly, would not heed remonstrance, was deluded by Bao Si, deposed his rightful queen; the deposed queen's father, Marquis of Shen, joined Quanrong to attack and kill King You.

sentences.zh: 一曰，其在天文，水為辰星，辰星為蠻夷。

translations.literal: Another view: in astronomy, water is the planet Chenxing, and Chenxing represents barbarian peoples.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: in astronomy, water is the planet Chenxing, and Chenxing represents barbarian peoples.

sentences.zh: 月食辰星，國以女亡。

translations.literal: When the moon eclipses Chenxing, a state perishes through women.
translations.idiomatic: When the moon eclipses Chenxing, a state perishes through women.

sentences.zh: 幽王之敗，女亂其內，夷攻其外。

translations.literal: In King You's defeat, women caused disorder within and barbarians attacked from without.
translations.idiomatic: In King You's defeat, women caused disorder within and barbarians attacked from without.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「君臣相背，厥異名水絕。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ruler and ministers turn against each other, its anomaly is called waters cut off."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ruler and ministers turn against each other, its anomaly is called waters cut off."

sentences.zh: 文公九年「九月癸酉，地震」。

translations.literal: In Duke Wen year nine: "On guiyou day of the ninth month, earthquake."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Wen year nine: "On guiyou day of the ninth month, earthquake."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是時，齊桓、晉文、魯釐二伯賢君新沒，周襄王失道，楚穆王殺父，諸侯皆不肖，權傾於下，天戒若曰，臣下彊盛者將動為害。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this, the worthy hegemon-rulers Duke Huan of Qi, Duke Wen of Jin, and Duke Xi of Lu had newly died; King Xiang of Zhou had lost the Way; King Mu of Chu killed his father; the feudal lords were all unworthy and power tilted below. Heaven's warning was as if saying powerful ministers below would move to cause harm.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this, the worthy hegemon-rulers Duke Huan of Qi, Duke Wen of Jin, and Duke Xi of Lu had newly died; King Xiang of Zhou had lost the Way; King Mu of Chu killed his father; the feudal lords were all unworthy and power tilted below. Heaven's warning was as if saying powerful ministers below would move to cause harm.

sentences.zh: 後宋、魯、晉、莒、鄭、陳、齊皆殺君。

translations.literal: Afterward Song, Lu, Jin, Ju, Zheng, Chen, and Qi all killed their rulers.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Song, Lu, Jin, Ju, Zheng, Chen, and Qi all killed their rulers.

sentences.zh: 諸震，略皆從董仲舒說也。

translations.literal: Most earthquake cases generally follow Dong Zhongshu's explanation.
translations.idiomatic: Most earthquake cases generally follow Dong Zhongshu's explanation.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「臣事雖正，專必震，其震，於水則波，於木則搖，於屋則瓦落。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Even if a minister's service is formally proper, monopoly will still bring earthquake: in water it appears as waves, in wood as shaking, in houses as falling tiles.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "Even if a minister's service is formally proper, monopoly will still bring earthquake: in water it appears as waves, in wood as shaking, in houses as falling tiles.

sentences.zh: 大經在辟而易臣，茲謂陰動，厥震搖政宮。

translations.literal: When the great norm lies with the ruler yet is shifted to ministers, this is called yin moving; its quake shakes government palaces.
translations.idiomatic: When the great norm lies with the ruler yet is shifted to ministers, this is called yin moving; its quake shakes government palaces.

sentences.zh: 大經搖政，茲謂不陰，厥震搖山，山出涌水。

translations.literal: When the great norm of government is shaken, this is called non-yin; its quake shakes mountains, and surging waters emerge from mountains.
translations.idiomatic: When the great norm of government is shaken, this is called non-yin; its quake shakes mountains, and surging waters emerge from mountains.

sentences.zh: 嗣子無德專祿，茲謂不順，厥震動丘陵，涌水出。」

translations.literal: When an heir-son lacking virtue monopolizes emolument, this is called noncompliance; its quake moves hills and ridges and surging waters emerge."
translations.idiomatic: When an heir-son lacking virtue monopolizes emolument, this is called noncompliance; its quake moves hills and ridges and surging waters emerge."

sentences.zh: 襄公十六年「五月甲子，地震」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xiang year sixteen: "On jiazi day of the fifth month, earthquake."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xiang year sixteen: "On jiazi day of the fifth month, earthquake."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為先是雞澤之會，諸侯盟，大夫又盟。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that before this there was the assembly at Jize, where feudal lords covenanted and great officers also covenanted.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that before this there was the assembly at Jize, where feudal lords covenanted and great officers also covenanted.

sentences.zh: 是歲三月，諸侯為溴梁之會，而大夫獨相與盟，五月地震矣。

translations.literal: That year in third month, at the Chouliang assembly of feudal lords, the great officers alone covenanted with one another; by fifth month there was earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: That year in third month, at the Chouliang assembly of feudal lords, the great officers alone covenanted with one another; by fifth month there was earthquake.

sentences.zh: 其後崔氏專齊，欒盈亂晉，良霄傾鄭，閽殺吳子，燕逐其君，楚滅陳、蔡。

translations.literal: Afterward the Cui clan monopolized Qi, Luan Ying threw Jin into disorder, Liang Xiao upended Zheng, gatekeepers killed the ruler of Wu, Yan expelled its ruler, and Chu destroyed Chen and Cai.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the Cui clan monopolized Qi, Luan Ying threw Jin into disorder, Liang Xiao upended Zheng, gatekeepers killed the ruler of Wu, Yan expelled its ruler, and Chu destroyed Chen and Cai.

sentences.zh: 昭公十九年「五月己卯，地震」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhao year nineteen: "On jimao day of fifth month, earthquake."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhao year nineteen: "On jimao day of fifth month, earthquake."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為是時季氏將有逐君之變。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that at this time the Ji clan was about to produce the upheaval of expelling the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that at this time the Ji clan was about to produce the upheaval of expelling the ruler.

sentences.zh: 其後宋三臣、曹會皆以地叛，蔡、莒逐其君，吳敗中國殺二君。

translations.literal: Afterward three ministers in Song and Hui of Cao all rebelled with territory; Cai and Ju expelled their rulers; Wu defeated the central states and killed two rulers.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward three ministers in Song and Hui of Cao all rebelled with territory; Cai and Ju expelled their rulers; Wu defeated the central states and killed two rulers.

sentences.zh: 二十三年「八月乙未，地震」。

translations.literal: In year twenty-three: "On yiwei day of eighth month, earthquake."
translations.idiomatic: In year twenty-three: "On yiwei day of eighth month, earthquake."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為是時周景王崩，劉、單立王子猛，尹氏立子朝。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that at this time King Jing of Zhou died, the Liu and Shan line installed Prince Meng, and the Yin clan installed Prince Chao.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that at this time King Jing of Zhou died, the Liu and Shan line installed Prince Meng, and the Yin clan installed Prince Chao.

sentences.zh: 其後季氏逐昭公，黑肱叛邾，吳殺其君僚，宋五大夫、晉二大夫皆以地叛。

translations.literal: Afterward the Ji clan expelled Duke Zhao; Heigong rebelled in Zhu; Wu killed its ruler Liao; five grandees of Song and two grandees of Jin all rebelled with territory.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the Ji clan expelled Duke Zhao; Heigong rebelled in Zhu; Wu killed its ruler Liao; five grandees of Song and two grandees of Jin all rebelled with territory.

sentences.zh: 哀公三年「四月甲午，地震」。

translations.literal: In Duke Ai year three: "On jiawu day of fourth month, earthquake."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Ai year three: "On jiawu day of fourth month, earthquake."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為是時諸侯皆信邪臣，莫能用仲尼，盜殺蔡侯，齊陳乞弒君。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that at this time all feudal lords trusted wicked ministers and none could employ Confucius; the marquis of Cai was assassinated, and Chen Qi of Qi regicide-killed his ruler.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that at this time all feudal lords trusted wicked ministers and none could employ Confucius; the marquis of Cai was assassinated, and Chen Qi of Qi regicide-killed his ruler.

sentences.zh: 惠帝二年正月，地震隴西，厭四百餘家。

translations.literal: In first month of Emperor Hui year two, earthquake struck Longxi, burying over four hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: In first month of Emperor Hui year two, earthquake struck Longxi, burying over four hundred households.

sentences.zh: 武帝征和二年八月癸亥，地震，厭殺人。

translations.literal: On guihai day of eighth month, Zhenghe year two under Emperor Wu, earthquake struck and crushed people to death.
translations.idiomatic: On guihai day of eighth month, Zhenghe year two under Emperor Wu, earthquake struck and crushed people to death.

sentences.zh: 宣帝本始四年四月壬寅，地震河南以東四十九郡，北海琅邪壞祖宗廟城郭，殺六千餘人。

translations.literal: On renyin day of the fourth month, Benshi year four under Emperor Xuan, earthquakes struck forty-nine commanderies east of Henan; in Beihai and Langya, ancestral temples and city walls collapsed, killing over six thousand people.
translations.idiomatic: On renyin day of the fourth month, Benshi year four under Emperor Xuan, earthquakes struck forty-nine commanderies east of Henan; in Beihai and Langya, ancestral temples and city walls collapsed, killing over six thousand people.

sentences.zh: 元帝永光三年冬，地震。

translations.literal: In winter of Yongguang year three under Emperor Yuan, earthquake occurred.
translations.idiomatic: In winter of Yongguang year three under Emperor Yuan, earthquake occurred.

sentences.zh: 綏和二年九月丙辰，地震，自京師至北邊郡國三十餘壞城郭，凡殺四百一十五人。

translations.literal: On bingchen day of ninth month, Suihe year two, earthquake from the capital to northern border commanderies and kingdoms damaged over thirty city walls, killing 415 people in total.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen day of ninth month, Suihe year two, earthquake from the capital to northern border commanderies and kingdoms damaged over thirty city walls, killing 415 people in total.

sentences.zh: 釐公十四年「秋八月辛卯，沙麓崩」。

translations.literal: In Duke Xi year fourteen: "On xinmao day of the eighth month in autumn, Shalu collapsed."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Xi year fourteen: "On xinmao day of the eighth month in autumn, Shalu collapsed."

sentences.zh: 穀梁傳曰：「林屬於山曰麓，沙其名也。」

translations.literal: The Guliang Tradition says: "The woods attached to a mountain are called lu; Sha is its name."
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang Tradition says: "The woods attached to a mountain are called lu; Sha is its name."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為臣下背叛，散落不事上之象也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held this symbolized ministers below betraying and scattering, not serving above.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held this symbolized ministers below betraying and scattering, not serving above.

sentences.zh: 先是，齊桓行伯道，會諸侯，事周室。

translations.literal: Before this, Duke Huan of Qi practiced hegemonic leadership, convened feudal lords, and served the Zhou house.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Duke Huan of Qi practiced hegemonic leadership, convened feudal lords, and served the Zhou house.

sentences.zh: 管仲既死，桓德日衰，天戒若曰，伯道將廢，諸侯散落，政逮大夫，陪臣執命，臣下不事上矣。

translations.literal: After Guan Zhong died, Duke Huan's virtue declined daily. Heaven's warning was as if saying the hegemonic way would be abolished, feudal lords would scatter, governance would reach great officers, attendant ministers would hold command, and ministers below would not serve above.
translations.idiomatic: After Guan Zhong died, Duke Huan's virtue declined daily. Heaven's warning was as if saying the hegemonic way would be abolished, feudal lords would scatter, governance would reach great officers, attendant ministers would hold command, and ministers below would not serve above.

sentences.zh: 桓公不寤，天子蔽晦。

translations.literal: Duke Huan did not awaken, and the Son of Heaven became obscured.
translations.idiomatic: Duke Huan did not awaken, and the Son of Heaven became obscured.

sentences.zh: 及齊威死，天下散而從楚。

translations.literal: When Duke Wei of Qi died, the realm scattered and followed Chu.
translations.idiomatic: When Duke Wei of Qi died, the realm scattered and followed Chu.

sentences.zh: 王札子殺二大夫，晉敗天子之師，莫能征討，從是陵遲。

translations.literal: Prince Zha killed two grandees; Jin defeated the Son of Heaven's army; none could punish them, and from there decline accelerated.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Zha killed two grandees; Jin defeated the Son of Heaven's army; none could punish them, and from there decline accelerated.

sentences.zh: 公羊以為沙麓，河上邑也。

translations.literal: The Gongyang tradition takes Shalu to be a riverside settlement.
translations.idiomatic: The Gongyang tradition takes Shalu to be a riverside settlement.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒說略同。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu's explanation is broadly similar.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu's explanation is broadly similar.

sentences.zh: 一曰，河，大川象；

translations.literal: Another view: the River symbolizes great rivers;
translations.idiomatic: Another view: the River symbolizes great rivers;

sentences.zh: 齊，大國；

translations.literal: Qi symbolizes a great state;
translations.idiomatic: Qi symbolizes a great state;

sentences.zh: 桓德衰，伯道將移於晉文，故河為徙也。

translations.literal: with Duke Huan's virtue declining, hegemony would shift to Duke Wen of Jin, therefore the River was shown as moving.
translations.idiomatic: with Duke Huan's virtue declining, hegemony would shift to Duke Wen of Jin, therefore the River was shown as moving.

sentences.zh: 左氏以為沙麓，晉地；

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition takes Shalu as Jin territory;
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition takes Shalu as Jin territory;

sentences.zh: 沙，山名也；

translations.literal: Sha is a mountain name;
translations.idiomatic: Sha is a mountain name;

sentences.zh: 地震而麓崩，不書震，舉重者也。

translations.literal: the earth quaked and the foothill collapsed; earthquake is not written because the graver sign is recorded.
translations.idiomatic: the earth quaked and the foothill collapsed; earthquake is not written because the graver sign is recorded.

sentences.zh: 伯陽甫所謂「

translations.literal: As Boyang Fu said, "
translations.idiomatic: As Boyang Fu said, "

sentences.zh: 國必依山川，山崩川竭，亡之徵也；

translations.literal: A state must rely on mountains and rivers; mountain collapse and river exhaustion are signs of ruin;
translations.idiomatic: A state must rely on mountains and rivers; mountain collapse and river exhaustion are signs of ruin;

sentences.zh: 不過十年，數之紀也。」

translations.literal: within no more than ten years, by the cycle of numbers."
translations.idiomatic: within no more than ten years, by the cycle of numbers."

sentences.zh: 至二十四年，晉懷公殺於高梁。

translations.literal: By year twenty-four, Duke Huai of Jin was killed at Gaoliang.
translations.idiomatic: By year twenty-four, Duke Huai of Jin was killed at Gaoliang.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「小人剝廬，厥妖山崩，茲謂陰乘陽，弱勝彊。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When petty men strip the house, its portent is mountain collapse; this is called yin riding yang, the weak overcoming the strong."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When petty men strip the house, its portent is mountain collapse; this is called yin riding yang, the weak overcoming the strong."

sentences.zh: 成公五年「夏，梁山崩」。

translations.literal: In Duke Cheng year five: "In summer, Mount Liang collapsed."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Cheng year five: "In summer, Mount Liang collapsed."

sentences.zh: 穀梁傳曰廱河三日不流，晉君帥群臣而哭之，乃流。

translations.literal: The Guliang Tradition says the Yong River stopped flowing for three days; the ruler of Jin led all ministers in mourning cries, and only then it flowed.
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang Tradition says the Yong River stopped flowing for three days; the ruler of Jin led all ministers in mourning cries, and only then it flowed.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為山陽，君也，水陰，民也，天戒若曰，君道崩壞，下亂，百姓將失其所矣。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held: mountain is yang, ruler; water is yin, people. Heaven's warning was as if saying rulerly way is collapsing, below is in disorder, and the people will lose their place.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held: mountain is yang, ruler; water is yin, people. Heaven's warning was as if saying rulerly way is collapsing, below is in disorder, and the people will lose their place.

sentences.zh: 哭然後流，喪亡象也。

translations.literal: Only after they wept did the river flow again; this is an image of mourning and loss.
translations.idiomatic: Only after they wept did the river flow again; this is an image of mourning and loss.

sentences.zh: 梁山在晉地，自晉始而及天下也。

translations.literal: Mount Liang was in Jin territory, beginning with Jin and extending to all under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Mount Liang was in Jin territory, beginning with Jin and extending to all under heaven.

sentences.zh: 後晉暴殺三卿，厲公以弒。

translations.literal: Later Jin violently killed the three high ministers; Duke Li was himself slain by regicide.
translations.idiomatic: Later Jin violently killed the three high ministers; Duke Li was himself slain by regicide.

sentences.zh: 溴梁之會，天下大夫皆執國政，其後孫、甯出衛獻，三家逐魯昭，單、尹亂王室。

translations.literal: At the Chouliang assembly, great officers across the realm all held state power; afterward Sun and Ning drove out Duke Xian of Wei, the Three Families expelled Duke Zhao of Lu, and Shan and Yin threw the royal house into chaos.
translations.idiomatic: At the Chouliang assembly, great officers across the realm all held state power; afterward Sun and Ning drove out Duke Xian of Wei, the Three Families expelled Duke Zhao of Lu, and Shan and Yin threw the royal house into chaos.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒說略同。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu's explanation was broadly similar.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu's explanation was broadly similar.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為梁山，晉望也；

translations.literal: Liu Xin held Mount Liang was a sacred peak of Jin;
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held Mount Liang was a sacred peak of Jin;

sentences.zh: 崩，弛崩也。

translations.literal: collapse means loosening collapse.
translations.idiomatic: collapse means loosening collapse.

sentences.zh: 古者三代命祀，祭不越望，吉凶禍福，不是過也。

translations.literal: In antiquity, with sacrificial mandates of the three dynasties, offerings did not pass beyond one's sacred bounds; auspicious and inauspicious fortune and calamity did not go beyond this.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity, with sacrificial mandates of the three dynasties, offerings did not pass beyond one's sacred bounds; auspicious and inauspicious fortune and calamity did not go beyond this.

sentences.zh: 國主山川，山崩川竭，亡之徵也，美惡周必復。

translations.literal: A state is ruled through mountains and rivers; mountain collapse and river exhaustion are signs of ruin. Good and bad cycles must return.
translations.idiomatic: A state is ruled through mountains and rivers; mountain collapse and river exhaustion are signs of ruin. Good and bad cycles must return.

sentences.zh: 是歲歲在鶉火，至十七年復在鶉火，欒書、中行偃殺厲公而立悼公。

translations.literal: That year Jupiter was in Quail Fire; in year seventeen it returned to Quail Fire, and Luan Shu and Zhonghang Yan killed Duke Li and installed Duke Dao.
translations.idiomatic: That year Jupiter was in Quail Fire; in year seventeen it returned to Quail Fire, and Luan Shu and Zhonghang Yan killed Duke Li and installed Duke Dao.

sentences.zh: 成帝河平三年二月丙戌，犍為柏江山崩，捐江山崩，皆廱江水，江水逆流壞城，殺十三人，地震積二十一日，百二十四動。

translations.literal: On bingxu day of second month, Heping year three under Emperor Cheng, Mount Baijiang in Qianwei collapsed and Mount Juanjiang collapsed; both blocked river waters, causing reverse flow that damaged cities and killed thirteen people. Earthquakes continued for twenty-one days with 124 tremors.
translations.idiomatic: On bingxu day of second month, Heping year three under Emperor Cheng, Mount Baijiang in Qianwei collapsed and Mount Juanjiang collapsed; both blocked river waters, causing reverse flow that damaged cities and killed thirteen people. Earthquakes continued for twenty-one days with 124 tremors.

sentences.zh: 元延三年正月丙寅，蜀郡岷山崩，廱江，江水逆流，三日乃通。

translations.literal: On bingyin day of first month, Yuanyan year three, Mount Min in Shu commandery collapsed, blocking the river; waters flowed backward and only after three days were passable.
translations.idiomatic: On bingyin day of first month, Yuanyan year three, Mount Min in Shu commandery collapsed, blocking the river; waters flowed backward and only after three days were passable.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為周時岐山崩，三川竭，而幽王亡。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that in Zhou times Mount Qi collapsed and the three rivers dried, after which King You perished.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that in Zhou times Mount Qi collapsed and the three rivers dried, after which King You perished.

sentences.zh: 岐山者，周所興也。

translations.literal: Mount Qi was where Zhou had arisen.
translations.idiomatic: Mount Qi was where Zhou had arisen.

sentences.zh: 漢家本起於蜀漢，今所起之地山崩川竭，星孛又及攝提、大角，從參至辰，殆必亡矣。

translations.literal: Han originally arose from Shu-Han; now in the place of its rise, mountains collapse and rivers dry, and comet signs also reached Sheti and Dajiao, from Shen to Chen. It almost certainly means extinction.
translations.idiomatic: Han originally arose from Shu-Han; now in the place of its rise, mountains collapse and rivers dry, and comet signs also reached Sheti and Dajiao, from Shen to Chen. It almost certainly means extinction.

sentences.zh: 其後三世亡嗣，王莽篡位。

translations.literal: Afterward, within three generations, the line lacked heirs and Wang Mang usurped the throne.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward, within three generations, the line lacked heirs and Wang Mang usurped the throne.

sentences.zh: 傳曰：「皇之不極，是謂不建，厥咎眊，厥罰恆陰，厥極弱。

translations.literal: The Tradition says: "When the ruler does not reach the ultimate mean, this is called not establishing; its blame is dim confusion, its punishment is constant overcast, and its extreme is weakness.
translations.idiomatic: The Tradition says: "When the ruler does not reach the ultimate mean, this is called not establishing; its blame is dim confusion, its punishment is constant overcast, and its extreme is weakness.

sentences.zh: 時則有射妖，時則有龍蛇之孽，時則有馬禍，時則有下人伐上之痾，時則有日月亂行，星辰逆行。」

translations.literal: Then at times there are archery anomalies, at times prodigies of dragons and snakes, at times horse calamities, at times afflictions of inferiors attacking superiors, and at times sun and moon move disorderly and stars move in reverse."
translations.idiomatic: Then at times there are archery anomalies, at times prodigies of dragons and snakes, at times horse calamities, at times afflictions of inferiors attacking superiors, and at times sun and moon move disorderly and stars move in reverse."

sentences.zh: 「皇之不極，是謂不建」，皇，君也。

translations.literal: "Ruler not reaching the ultimate, this is called not establishing"; huang means ruler.
translations.idiomatic: "Ruler not reaching the ultimate, this is called not establishing"; huang means ruler.

sentences.zh: 極，中；

translations.literal: Ultimate means center;
translations.idiomatic: Ultimate means center;

sentences.zh: 建，立也。

translations.literal: establish means to set up.
translations.idiomatic: establish means to set up.

sentences.zh: 人君貌言視聽思心五事皆失，不得其中，則不能立萬事，失在眊悖，故其咎眊也。

translations.literal: If a ruler's five affairs of bearing, speech, sight, hearing, and thought all fail and miss the center, he cannot establish the myriad affairs. The fault lies in blurred perversity, so its blame is dim confusion.
translations.idiomatic: If a ruler's five affairs of bearing, speech, sight, hearing, and thought all fail and miss the center, he cannot establish the myriad affairs. The fault lies in blurred perversity, so its blame is dim confusion.

sentences.zh: 王者自下承天理物。

translations.literal: A true king receives from below and manages things by Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: A true king receives from below and manages things by Heaven.

sentences.zh: 雲起於山，而彌於天；

translations.literal: Clouds rise from mountains and spread across the sky.
translations.idiomatic: Clouds rise from mountains and spread across the sky.

sentences.zh: 天氣亂，故其罰常陰也。

translations.literal: If heavenly qi is disordered, therefore the punishment is constant overcast.
translations.idiomatic: If heavenly qi is disordered, therefore the punishment is constant overcast.

sentences.zh: 一曰，上失中，則下彊盛而蔽君明也。

translations.literal: Another view: if those above lose the center, those below become strong and obscure rulerly clarity.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: if those above lose the center, those below become strong and obscure rulerly clarity.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰「亢龍有悔，貴而亡位，高而亡民，賢人在下位而亡輔」，如此，則君有南面之尊，而亡一人之助，故其極弱也。

translations.literal: The Changes says, "Overbearing dragon has regret: noble yet without position, high yet without people, worthy men below without support." Thus though the ruler has the honored south-facing seat, he lacks the aid of even one person, and therefore the extreme is weakness.
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says, "Overbearing dragon has regret: noble yet without position, high yet without people, worthy men below without support." Thus though the ruler has the honored south-facing seat, he lacks the aid of even one person, and therefore the extreme is weakness.

sentences.zh: 盛陽動進輕疾。

translations.literal: Flourishing yang moves forward lightly and swiftly.
translations.idiomatic: Flourishing yang moves forward lightly and swiftly.

sentences.zh: 禮，春而大射，以順陽氣。

translations.literal: Ritually, in spring one holds the great archery ceremony to accord with yang qi.
translations.idiomatic: Ritually, in spring one holds the great archery ceremony to accord with yang qi.

sentences.zh: 上微弱則下奮動，故有射妖。

translations.literal: If those above are weak, those below surge into action; therefore there are archery anomalies.
translations.idiomatic: If those above are weak, those below surge into action; therefore there are archery anomalies.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰「雲從龍」，又曰「龍蛇之蟄，以存身也」。

translations.literal: The Changes says, "Clouds follow dragon," and also, "Dragon and snake hibernate to preserve themselves."
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says, "Clouds follow dragon," and also, "Dragon and snake hibernate to preserve themselves."

sentences.zh: 陰氣動，故有龍蛇之孽。

translations.literal: When yin qi stirs, therefore there are prodigies of dragons and snakes.
translations.idiomatic: When yin qi stirs, therefore there are prodigies of dragons and snakes.

sentences.zh: 於易，乾為君為馬，馬任用而彊力，君氣毀，故有馬禍。

translations.literal: In the Changes, Qian is ruler and horse; horses bear service and great force. When ruler-qi is damaged, therefore there are horse calamities.
translations.idiomatic: In the Changes, Qian is ruler and horse; horses bear service and great force. When ruler-qi is damaged, therefore there are horse calamities.

sentences.zh: 一曰，馬多死及為怪，亦是也。

translations.literal: Another view: many horses dying or becoming strange also belongs here.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: many horses dying or becoming strange also belongs here.

sentences.zh: 君亂且弱，人之所叛，天之所去，不有明王之誅，則有篡弒之禍，故有下人伐上之痾。

translations.literal: When the ruler is chaotic and weak, people rebel and Heaven abandons. Without execution by a manifest king, there is disaster of usurpation and regicide; therefore there are afflictions of inferiors attacking superiors.
translations.idiomatic: When the ruler is chaotic and weak, people rebel and Heaven abandons. Without execution by a manifest king, there is disaster of usurpation and regicide; therefore there are afflictions of inferiors attacking superiors.

sentences.zh: 凡君道傷者病天氣，不言五行沴天，而曰「日月亂行，星辰逆行」者，為若下不敢沴天，猶春秋曰「王師敗績于貿戎」，不言敗之者，以自敗為文，尊尊之意也。

translations.literal: Whenever rulerly way is harmed, heavenly qi is diseased. It does not say five phases afflict Heaven, but says sun and moon move disorderly and stars in reverse, because inferiors dare not be said to afflict Heaven, just as Spring and Autumn says "the royal army was defeated at Mao-Rong" without naming who defeated it, expressing reverence for the honored.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever rulerly way is harmed, heavenly qi is diseased. It does not say five phases afflict Heaven, but says sun and moon move disorderly and stars in reverse, because inferiors dare not be said to afflict Heaven, just as Spring and Autumn says "the royal army was defeated at Mao-Rong" without naming who defeated it, expressing reverence for the honored.

sentences.zh: 劉歆皇極傳曰有下體生上之痾。

translations.literal: Liu Xin's Huangji transmission says there are afflictions of lower body growing above.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin's Huangji transmission says there are afflictions of lower body growing above.

sentences.zh: 說以為下人伐上，天誅已成，不得復為痾云。

translations.literal: His explanation says inferiors attacking superiors means Heaven's punishment already complete, so it should not again be called an affliction.
translations.idiomatic: His explanation says inferiors attacking superiors means Heaven's punishment already complete, so it should not again be called an affliction.

sentences.zh: 皇極之常陰，劉向以為春秋亡其應。

translations.literal: For the regular overcast of Huangji, Liu Xiang held Spring and Autumn has no matching case.
translations.idiomatic: For the regular overcast of Huangji, Liu Xiang held Spring and Autumn has no matching case.

sentences.zh: 一曰，久陰不雨是也。

translations.literal: Another view: prolonged overcast without rain is it.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: prolonged overcast without rain is it.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為自屬常陰。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held it belongs to constant overcast.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held it belongs to constant overcast.

sentences.zh: 嚴公十八年「秋，有蜮」。

translations.literal: In Duke Zhuang year eighteen: "In autumn, there were yu insects."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Zhuang year eighteen: "In autumn, there were yu insects."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為蜮生南越。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held yu were born in Nanyue.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held yu were born in Nanyue.

sentences.zh: 越地多婦人，男女同川，淫女為主，亂氣所生，故聖人名之曰蜮。

translations.literal: In Yue lands there were many women; men and women mingled at the rivers, with licentious women taking the lead. From this disordered qi the creature was said to arise, and so sages named it yu.
translations.idiomatic: In Yue lands there were many women; men and women mingled at the rivers, with licentious women taking the lead. From this disordered qi the creature was said to arise, and so sages named it yu.

sentences.zh: 蜮猶惑也，在水旁，能射人，射人有處，甚者至死。

translations.literal: Yu is akin to confusion: it lurks by water and can shoot at people; where it strikes, lesions appear, and severe cases can be fatal.
translations.idiomatic: Yu is akin to confusion: it lurks by water and can shoot at people; where it strikes, lesions appear, and severe cases can be fatal.

sentences.zh: 南方謂之短弧，近射妖，死亡之象也。

translations.literal: In the south it is called short-bow, near an archery anomaly, image of death.
translations.idiomatic: In the south it is called short-bow, near an archery anomaly, image of death.

sentences.zh: 時嚴將取齊之淫女，故蜮至。

translations.literal: At that time Duke Zhuang was about to take a licentious woman of Qi, so yu arrived.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Duke Zhuang was about to take a licentious woman of Qi, so yu arrived.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，勿取齊女，將生淫惑篡弒之禍。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not take the woman of Qi, or there will be calamity of licentious confusion, usurpation, and regicide.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not take the woman of Qi, or there will be calamity of licentious confusion, usurpation, and regicide.

sentences.zh: 嚴不寤，遂取之。

translations.literal: Zhuang did not awaken and took her anyway.
translations.idiomatic: Zhuang did not awaken and took her anyway.

sentences.zh: 入後淫於二叔，二叔以死，兩子見弒，夫人亦誅。

translations.literal: After she entered, she had illicit relations with two uncles; those two uncles died, two sons were slain, and the wife too was executed.
translations.idiomatic: After she entered, she had illicit relations with two uncles; those two uncles died, two sons were slain, and the wife too was executed.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為蜮，盛暑所生，非自越來也。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held yu are generated by intense summer heat, not coming from Yue.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held yu are generated by intense summer heat, not coming from Yue.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「忠臣進善君不試，厥咎國生蜮。」
