translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When loyal ministers advance good counsel and the ruler does not test it, its blame-sign is the state producing yu."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When loyal ministers advance good counsel and the ruler does not test it, its blame-sign is the state producing yu."

sentences.zh: 史記魯哀公時，有隼集于陳廷而死，楛矢貫之，石砮，長尺有咫。

translations.literal: The Records says that in Duke Ai of Lu's time, a falcon landed in the Chen court and died, pierced by a mulberry-wood arrow with stone point, one chi plus a zhi in length.
translations.idiomatic: The Records says that in Duke Ai of Lu's time, a falcon landed in the Chen court and died, pierced by a mulberry-wood arrow with stone point, one chi plus a zhi in length.

sentences.zh: 陳閔公使使問仲尼，仲尼曰：「

translations.literal: Duke Min of Chen sent an envoy to ask Confucius, and Confucius said: "
translations.idiomatic: Duke Min of Chen sent an envoy to ask Confucius, and Confucius said: "

sentences.zh: 隼之來遠矣！

translations.literal: This falcon has come from far away!
translations.idiomatic: This falcon has come from far away!

sentences.zh: 昔武王克商，通道百蠻，使各以方物來貢，肅慎貢楛矢，石砮長尺有咫。

translations.literal: Formerly when King Wu conquered Shang, he opened routes to the hundred southern tribes and had each present regional tribute. The Sushen offered mulberry arrows and stone points one chi plus a zhi long.
translations.idiomatic: Formerly when King Wu conquered Shang, he opened routes to the hundred southern tribes and had each present regional tribute. The Sushen offered mulberry arrows and stone points one chi plus a zhi long.

sentences.zh: 先王分異姓以遠方職，使毋忘服，故分陳以肅慎矢。」

translations.literal: The former kings apportioned different surnames to distant offices so they would not forget submission; therefore they assigned Chen with Sushen arrows."
translations.idiomatic: The former kings apportioned different surnames to distant offices so they would not forget submission; therefore they assigned Chen with Sushen arrows."

sentences.zh: 試求之故府，果得之。

translations.literal: They searched the old treasury and indeed found them.
translations.idiomatic: They searched the old treasury and indeed found them.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為隼近黑祥，貪暴類也；

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held the falcon was a near black portent, of greedy and violent kind;
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held the falcon was a near black portent, of greedy and violent kind;

sentences.zh: 矢貫之，近射妖也；

translations.literal: the arrow piercing it was near an archery anomaly;
translations.idiomatic: the arrow piercing it was near an archery anomaly;

sentences.zh: 死於廷，國亡表也。

translations.literal: its death in the court was a sign of state ruin.
translations.idiomatic: its death in the court was a sign of state ruin.

sentences.zh: 象陳眊亂，不服事周，而行貪暴，將致遠夷之禍，為所滅也。

translations.literal: It symbolized Chen's confused disorder: failing to serve Zhou and practicing greed and violence, it would bring calamity from distant tribes and be destroyed by them.
translations.idiomatic: It symbolized Chen's confused disorder: failing to serve Zhou and practicing greed and violence, it would bring calamity from distant tribes and be destroyed by them.

sentences.zh: 是時中國齊晉、南夷吳楚為彊，陳交晉不親，附楚不固，數被二國之禍。

translations.literal: At that time among the central states Qi and Jin were strong, and among southern tribes Wu and Chu were strong. Chen, between them, was not close to Jin nor firm with Chu, and repeatedly suffered the two states' calamities.
translations.idiomatic: At that time among the central states Qi and Jin were strong, and among southern tribes Wu and Chu were strong. Chen, between them, was not close to Jin nor firm with Chu, and repeatedly suffered the two states' calamities.

sentences.zh: 後楚有白公之亂，陳乘而侵之，卒為楚所滅。

translations.literal: Later when Chu had the disorder of Bai Gong, Chen took advantage and invaded it, but eventually was destroyed by Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Later when Chu had the disorder of Bai Gong, Chen took advantage and invaded it, but eventually was destroyed by Chu.

sentences.zh: 史記夏后氏之衰，有二龍止於夏廷，而言「余，褒之二君也」。

translations.literal: The Records says that in the decline of Xia there were two dragons that stopped in Xia's court and said, "We are two rulers of Bao."
translations.idiomatic: The Records says that in the decline of Xia there were two dragons that stopped in Xia's court and said, "We are two rulers of Bao."

sentences.zh: 夏帝卜殺之，去之，止之，莫吉；

translations.literal: The Xia ruler divined to kill them, drive them off, or keep them, but none was auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: The Xia ruler divined to kill them, drive them off, or keep them, but none was auspicious.

sentences.zh: 卜請其漦而藏之，乃吉。

translations.literal: He then divined requesting their spittle to store; only then was it auspicious.
translations.idiomatic: He then divined requesting their spittle to store; only then was it auspicious.

sentences.zh: 於是布幣策告之。

translations.literal: Thereupon offerings and written notice were presented.
translations.idiomatic: Thereupon offerings and written notice were presented.

sentences.zh: 龍亡而漦在，乃櫝去之。

translations.literal: The dragons vanished but the spittle remained, so they put it in a casket and removed it.
translations.idiomatic: The dragons vanished but the spittle remained, so they put it in a casket and removed it.

sentences.zh: 其後夏亡，傳櫝於殷周，三代莫發，至厲王末，發而觀之，漦流于廷，不可除也。

translations.literal: After Xia fell, the casket was transmitted to Shang and Zhou; none of the three dynasties opened it. By the end of King Li, it was opened and viewed, and the spittle flowed in the court, impossible to clear away.
translations.idiomatic: After Xia fell, the casket was transmitted to Shang and Zhou; none of the three dynasties opened it. By the end of King Li, it was opened and viewed, and the spittle flowed in the court, impossible to clear away.

sentences.zh: 厲王使婦人臝而譟之，漦化為玄黿，入後宮。

translations.literal: King Li had women strip and shout at it; the spittle transformed into a dark lizard and entered the harem.
translations.idiomatic: King Li had women strip and shout at it; the spittle transformed into a dark lizard and entered the harem.

sentences.zh: 處妾遇之而孕，生子，懼而棄之。

translations.literal: A palace woman encountered it and became pregnant; she gave birth to a child, feared it, and abandoned it.
translations.idiomatic: A palace woman encountered it and became pregnant; she gave birth to a child, feared it, and abandoned it.

sentences.zh: 宣王立，女童謠曰：「览弧萁服，實亡周國。」

translations.literal: When King Xuan took the throne, girls sang a rhyme: "Mulberry bow and reed quiver will truly destroy Zhou."
translations.idiomatic: When King Xuan took the throne, girls sang a rhyme: "Mulberry bow and reed quiver will truly destroy Zhou."

sentences.zh: 後有夫婦鬻是器者，宣王使執而僇之。

translations.literal: Later a husband and wife selling those items appeared; King Xuan ordered them seized and executed.
translations.idiomatic: Later a husband and wife selling those items appeared; King Xuan ordered them seized and executed.

sentences.zh: 既去，見處妾所棄妖子，聞其夜號，哀而收之，遂亡奔褒。

translations.literal: After they had gone, they saw the abandoned uncanny child of that palace woman, heard its crying at night, pitied it, took it in, and fled to Bao.
translations.idiomatic: After they had gone, they saw the abandoned uncanny child of that palace woman, heard its crying at night, pitied it, took it in, and fled to Bao.

sentences.zh: 後褒人有罪，入妖子以贖，是為褒姒，幽王見而愛之，生子伯服。

translations.literal: Later, when Bao people had committed offense, they presented the uncanny child as ransom. This became Bao Si; King You saw and loved her, and she bore Bo Fu.
translations.idiomatic: Later, when Bao people had committed offense, they presented the uncanny child as ransom. This became Bao Si; King You saw and loved her, and she bore Bo Fu.

sentences.zh: 王廢申后及太子宜咎，而立褒姒、伯服代之。

translations.literal: The king deposed Empress Shen and crown prince Yijiu, and established Bao Si and Bo Fu in their place.
translations.idiomatic: The king deposed Empress Shen and crown prince Yijiu, and established Bao Si and Bo Fu in their place.

sentences.zh: 廢后之父申侯與繒西畎戎共攻殺幽王。

translations.literal: The deposed empress's father, Marquis of Shen, with Zeng and the western Quanrong, jointly attacked and killed King You.
translations.idiomatic: The deposed empress's father, Marquis of Shen, with Zeng and the western Quanrong, jointly attacked and killed King You.

sentences.zh: 《詩》曰：「赫赫宗周，褒姒醤之。」

translations.literal: The Odes says: "Brilliant ancestral Zhou, Bao Si destroyed it."
translations.idiomatic: The Odes says: "Brilliant ancestral Zhou, Bao Si destroyed it."

sentences.zh: 劉向以為夏后季世，周之幽、厲，皆誖亂逆天，故有龍黿之怪，近龍蛇孽也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that in late Xia and in Zhou under You and Li, all were perverse and against Heaven; therefore there were the strange dragon-lizard events, near prodigies of dragons and snakes.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that in late Xia and in Zhou under You and Li, all were perverse and against Heaven; therefore there were the strange dragon-lizard events, near prodigies of dragons and snakes.

sentences.zh: 漦，血也，一曰沫也。

translations.literal: Spittle means blood; another view says foam.
translations.idiomatic: Spittle means blood; another view says foam.

sentences.zh: 览弧，桑弓也。

translations.literal: Lan-bow means mulberry bow.
translations.idiomatic: Lan-bow means mulberry bow.

sentences.zh: 萁服，蓋以萁草為箭服，近射妖也。

translations.literal: Qi-quiver likely means arrow quiver made from qi grass, near an archery anomaly.
translations.idiomatic: Qi-quiver likely means arrow quiver made from qi grass, near an archery anomaly.

sentences.zh: 女童謠者，禍將生於女，國以兵寇亡也。

translations.literal: The girls' rhyme meant disaster would be born from a woman, and the state would perish through war and banditry.
translations.idiomatic: The girls' rhyme meant disaster would be born from a woman, and the state would perish through war and banditry.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳昭公十九年，龍鬥於鄭時門之外洧淵。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Zhao year nineteen, dragons fought outside Shi Gate of Zheng at the Wei abyss.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Zhao year nineteen, dragons fought outside Shi Gate of Zheng at the Wei abyss.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近龍孽也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near dragon prodigy.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near dragon prodigy.

sentences.zh: 鄭以小國攝乎晉楚之間，重以彊吳，鄭當其衝，不能修德，將鬥三國，以自危亡。

translations.literal: Zheng, a small state caught between Jin and Chu and also strong Wu, stood at the collision point. Unable to cultivate virtue, it would contend among three states and endanger itself.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng, a small state caught between Jin and Chu and also strong Wu, stood at the collision point. Unable to cultivate virtue, it would contend among three states and endanger itself.

sentences.zh: 是時子產任政，內惠於民，外善辭令，以交三國，鄭卒亡患，能以德消變之效也。

translations.literal: At that time Zichan governed affairs, beneficent to the people within and skillful in diplomacy without, engaging all three states; Zheng ultimately escaped disaster, effect of dispelling change through virtue.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Zichan governed affairs, beneficent to the people within and skillful in diplomacy without, engaging all three states; Zheng ultimately escaped disaster, effect of dispelling change through virtue.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「眾心不安，厥妖龍鬥。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When people's hearts are unsettled, its portent is dragons fighting."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When people's hearts are unsettled, its portent is dragons fighting."

sentences.zh: 惠帝二年正月癸酉旦，有兩龍見於蘭陵廷東里溫陵井中，至乙亥夜去。

translations.literal: At dawn on guiyou day of first month, Emperor Hui year two, two dragons appeared in Wenling Well in East Lane of Lanling court, and departed on the night of yihai.
translations.idiomatic: At dawn on guiyou day of first month, Emperor Hui year two, two dragons appeared in Wenling Well in East Lane of Lanling court, and departed on the night of yihai.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為龍貴象而困於庶人井中，象諸侯將有幽執之禍。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that dragons, symbols of nobility, trapped in a commoner's well symbolized feudal lords suffering imprisonment and death.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that dragons, symbols of nobility, trapped in a commoner's well symbolized feudal lords suffering imprisonment and death.

sentences.zh: 其後呂太后幽殺三趙王，諸呂亦終誅滅。

translations.literal: Afterward Empress Dowager Lü imprisoned and killed three kings of Zhao, and the Lü clans were ultimately all exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward Empress Dowager Lü imprisoned and killed three kings of Zhao, and the Lü clans were ultimately all exterminated.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「有德遭害，厥妖龍見井中。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the virtuous suffer harm, its portent is dragons appearing in a well."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the virtuous suffer harm, its portent is dragons appearing in a well."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「行刑暴惡，黑龍從井出。」

translations.literal: It also says: "When punishments are violent and evil, black dragons emerge from wells."
translations.idiomatic: It also says: "When punishments are violent and evil, black dragons emerge from wells."

sentences.zh: 左氏傳魯嚴公時有內蛇與外蛇鬥鄭南門中，內蛇死。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Zhuang of Lu's time, an inner snake and outer snake fought in Zheng's South Gate, and the inner snake died.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Zhuang of Lu's time, an inner snake and outer snake fought in Zheng's South Gate, and the inner snake died.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為近蛇孽也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang took this as a near snake prodigy.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took this as a near snake prodigy.

sentences.zh: 先是鄭厲公劫相祭仲而逐兄昭公代立。

translations.literal: Before this, Duke Li of Zheng coerced his minister Ji Zhong and expelled his elder brother Duke Zhao to take the throne.
translations.idiomatic: Before this, Duke Li of Zheng coerced his minister Ji Zhong and expelled his elder brother Duke Zhao to take the throne.

sentences.zh: 後厲公出奔，昭公復入。

translations.literal: Later Duke Li fled into exile and Duke Zhao returned.
translations.idiomatic: Later Duke Li fled into exile and Duke Zhao returned.

sentences.zh: 死，弟子儀代立。

translations.literal: After Zhao died, his younger brother Ziyi succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhao died, his younger brother Ziyi succeeded.

sentences.zh: 厲公自外劫大夫傅瑕，使僇子儀。

translations.literal: From abroad Duke Li coerced grandee Fu Xia to execute Ziyi.
translations.idiomatic: From abroad Duke Li coerced grandee Fu Xia to execute Ziyi.

sentences.zh: 此外蛇殺內蛇之象也。

translations.literal: This was the image of outer snake killing inner snake.
translations.idiomatic: This was the image of outer snake killing inner snake.

sentences.zh: 蛇死六年，而厲公立。

translations.literal: Six years after the snake's death, Duke Li was established.
translations.idiomatic: Six years after the snake's death, Duke Li was established.

sentences.zh: 嚴公聞之，問申繻曰：「猶有妖乎？」

translations.literal: Duke Zhuang heard this and asked Shen Xu: "Are there still portents?"
translations.idiomatic: Duke Zhuang heard this and asked Shen Xu: "Are there still portents?"

sentences.zh: 對曰：「人之所忌，其氣炎以取之，妖由人興也。

translations.literal: He replied: "What people fear, their heated qi reaches for; anomalies are stirred up by people themselves.
translations.idiomatic: He replied: "What people fear, their heated qi reaches for; anomalies are stirred up by people themselves.

sentences.zh: 人亡舋焉，妖不自作。

translations.literal: If people have no crack for them, anomalies do not make themselves.
translations.idiomatic: If people have no crack for them, anomalies do not make themselves.

sentences.zh: 人棄常，故有妖。」

translations.literal: When people abandon constancy, therefore anomalies occur."
translations.idiomatic: When people abandon constancy, therefore anomalies occur."

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「立嗣子疑，厥妖蛇居國門鬥。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the heir is set up in doubt, its portent is snakes fighting at the state gate."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the heir is set up in doubt, its portent is snakes fighting at the state gate."

sentences.zh: 武帝太始四年七月，趙有蛇從郭外入，與邑中蛇鬥孝文廟下，邑中蛇死。

translations.literal: In seventh month of Taishi year four under Emperor Wu, in Zhao a snake came in from outside city walls and fought snakes inside the district below the Temple of Emperor Xiaowen; the inner snakes died.
translations.idiomatic: In seventh month of Taishi year four under Emperor Wu, in Zhao a snake came in from outside city walls and fought snakes inside the district below the Temple of Emperor Xiaowen; the inner snakes died.

sentences.zh: 後二年秋，有衛太子事，事自趙人江充起。

translations.literal: Two years later, in autumn, there was the affair of the Crown Prince Wei, and the affair began from Jiang Chong of Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: Two years later, in autumn, there was the affair of the Crown Prince Wei, and the affair began from Jiang Chong of Zhao.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳定公十年，宋公子地有白馬駟，公嬖向魋欲之，公取而朱其尾鬣以予之。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Ding year ten: Prince Di of Song had a team of four white horses. The duke favored Xiang Tui and wanted them; the duke took them, painted their tails and manes red, and gave them to Xiang Tui.
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo Tradition says in Duke Ding year ten: Prince Di of Song had a team of four white horses. The duke favored Xiang Tui and wanted them; the duke took them, painted their tails and manes red, and gave them to Xiang Tui.

sentences.zh: 地怒，使其徒抶魋而奪之。

translations.literal: Di, enraged, had his followers beat Xiang Tui and seize them back.
translations.idiomatic: Di, enraged, had his followers beat Xiang Tui and seize them back.

sentences.zh: 魋懼將走，公閉門而泣之，目盡腫。

translations.literal: Xiang Tui, fearful, was about to flee; the duke shut the gate and wept for him until his eyes were swollen.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Tui, fearful, was about to flee; the duke shut the gate and wept for him until his eyes were swollen.

sentences.zh: 公弟辰謂地曰：「子為君禮，不過出竟，君必止子。」

translations.literal: The duke's younger brother Chen told Di: "If you observe ruler-minister propriety, do no more than leave the border; the ruler will surely stop you."
translations.idiomatic: The duke's younger brother Chen told Di: "If you observe ruler-minister propriety, do no more than leave the border; the ruler will surely stop you."

sentences.zh: 地出奔陳，公弗止。

translations.literal: Di fled to Chen; the duke did not stop him.
translations.idiomatic: Di fled to Chen; the duke did not stop him.

sentences.zh: 辰為之請，不聽。

translations.literal: Chen pleaded for him and was not heeded.
translations.idiomatic: Chen pleaded for him and was not heeded.

sentences.zh: 辰曰：「是我迋吾兄也，吾以國人出，君誰與處？」

translations.literal: Chen said: "Then I have deceived my elder brother. If I leave with the people of the state, with whom will the ruler remain?"
translations.idiomatic: Chen said: "Then I have deceived my elder brother. If I leave with the people of the state, with whom will the ruler remain?"

sentences.zh: 遂與其徒出奔陳。

translations.literal: So he and his followers also fled to Chen.
translations.idiomatic: So he and his followers also fled to Chen.

sentences.zh: 明年俱入于蕭以叛，大為宋患，近馬禍也。

translations.literal: The next year both entered Xiao and rebelled, causing great trouble for Song, a near horse calamity.
translations.idiomatic: The next year both entered Xiao and rebelled, causing great trouble for Song, a near horse calamity.

sentences.zh: 史記秦孝公二十一年有馬生人，昭王二十年牡馬生子而死。

translations.literal: The Records says: in year twenty-one of King Xiaogong of Qin, there was a horse giving birth to a human; in year twenty of King Zhao, a stallion gave birth and then died.
translations.idiomatic: The Records says: in year twenty-one of King Xiaogong of Qin, there was a horse giving birth to a human; in year twenty of King Zhao, a stallion gave birth and then died.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為皆馬禍也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang took both as horse calamities.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang took both as horse calamities.

sentences.zh: 孝公始用商君攻守之法，東侵諸侯，至於昭王，用兵彌烈。

translations.literal: Xiaogong first adopted Lord Shang's offensive-defensive laws and invaded feudal lords eastward; by King Zhao, warfare was even fiercer.
translations.idiomatic: Xiaogong first adopted Lord Shang's offensive-defensive laws and invaded feudal lords eastward; by King Zhao, warfare was even fiercer.

sentences.zh: 其象將以兵革抗極成功，而還自害也。

translations.literal: Its meaning is that they would push military force to the utmost and achieve success, only to destroy themselves in return.
translations.idiomatic: Its meaning is that they would push military force to the utmost and achieve success, only to destroy themselves in return.

sentences.zh: 牡馬非生類，妄生而死，猶秦恃力彊得天下，而還自滅之象也。

translations.literal: A stallion is not of birthing kind; to give birth rashly and die resembles Qin relying on force to win the realm and then destroying itself.
translations.idiomatic: A stallion is not of birthing kind; to give birth rashly and die resembles Qin relying on force to win the realm and then destroying itself.

sentences.zh: 曰，諸畜生非其類，子孫必有非其姓者，至於始皇，果呂不韋子。

translations.literal: Another saying: when livestock give birth outside their kind, descendants will include one not of their surname. By First Emperor, indeed he was Lü Buwei's son.
translations.idiomatic: Another saying: when livestock give birth outside their kind, descendants will include one not of their surname. By First Emperor, indeed he was Lü Buwei's son.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "

sentences.zh: 方伯分威，厥妖牡馬生子。

translations.literal: When regional lords divide authority, its portent is a stallion giving birth.
translations.idiomatic: When regional lords divide authority, its portent is a stallion giving birth.

sentences.zh: 亡天子，諸侯相伐，厥妖馬生人。」

translations.literal: When there is no Son of Heaven and feudal lords attack one another, its portent is horses giving birth to humans."
translations.idiomatic: When there is no Son of Heaven and feudal lords attack one another, its portent is horses giving birth to humans."

sentences.zh: 文帝十二年，有馬生角於吳，角在耳前，上鄉。

translations.literal: In Emperor Wen year twelve, in Wu, a horse grew horns, positioned before the ears and pointing upward.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Wen year twelve, in Wu, a horse grew horns, positioned before the ears and pointing upward.

sentences.zh: 右角長三寸，左角長二寸，皆大二寸。

translations.literal: The right horn was three cun long, the left two cun, both two cun thick.
translations.idiomatic: The right horn was three cun long, the left two cun, both two cun thick.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為馬不當生角，猶吳不當舉兵鄉上也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held a horse should not grow horns, just as Wu should not raise troops against the superior.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held a horse should not grow horns, just as Wu should not raise troops against the superior.

sentences.zh: 是時，吳王濞封有四郡五十餘城，內懷驕恣，變見於外，天戒早矣。

translations.literal: At that time King Pi of Wu held four commanderies and over fifty cities; inwardly he harbored arrogance and excess, and the change appeared outwardly. Heaven's warning came early.
translations.idiomatic: At that time King Pi of Wu held four commanderies and over fifty cities; inwardly he harbored arrogance and excess, and the change appeared outwardly. Heaven's warning came early.

sentences.zh: 王不寤，後卒舉兵，誅滅。

translations.literal: The king did not awaken; later he indeed raised troops and was executed and exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: The king did not awaken; later he indeed raised troops and was executed and exterminated.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「臣易上，政不順，厥妖馬生角，茲謂賢士不足。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ministers replace superiors and governance is noncompliant, its portent is horses growing horns; this is called lack of worthy men."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When ministers replace superiors and governance is noncompliant, its portent is horses growing horns; this is called lack of worthy men."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「天子親伐，馬生角。」

translations.literal: It also says: "When the Son of Heaven campaigns personally, horses grow horns."
translations.idiomatic: It also says: "When the Son of Heaven campaigns personally, horses grow horns."

sentences.zh: 成帝綏和三年二月，大廄馬生角，在左耳前，圍長各二寸。

translations.literal: In second month of Suihe year three under Emperor Cheng, a horse in the imperial stable grew horns before the left ear, each two cun around and long.
translations.idiomatic: In second month of Suihe year three under Emperor Cheng, a horse in the imperial stable grew horns before the left ear, each two cun around and long.

sentences.zh: 是時王莽為大司馬，害上之萌自此始矣。

translations.literal: At that time Wang Mang was Grand Marshal; from this point his harm to the throne first began.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Wang Mang was Grand Marshal; from this point his harm to the throne first began.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平二年，定襄牡馬生駒，三足，隨群飲食，太守以聞。

translations.literal: In Jianping year two under Emperor Ai, in Dingxiang a stallion gave birth to a colt with three legs that followed the herd in eating and drinking; the governor reported it.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianping year two under Emperor Ai, in Dingxiang a stallion gave birth to a colt with three legs that followed the herd in eating and drinking; the governor reported it.

sentences.zh: 馬，國之武用，三足，不任用之象也。

translations.literal: The horse is the state's instrument of war; having three legs symbolizes inability to serve effectively.
translations.idiomatic: The horse is the state's instrument of war; having three legs symbolizes inability to serve effectively.

sentences.zh: 後侍中董賢年二十二為大司馬，居上公之位，天下不宗。

translations.literal: Later Attendant-in-Ordinary Dong Xian, age twenty-two, became Grand Marshal and occupied the rank of highest duke, with no support under heaven.
translations.idiomatic: Later Attendant-in-Ordinary Dong Xian, age twenty-two, became Grand Marshal and occupied the rank of highest duke, with no support under heaven.

sentences.zh: 哀帝暴崩，成帝母王太后召弟子新都侯王莽入，收賢印綬，賢恐，自殺，莽因代之，並誅外家丁、傅。

translations.literal: Emperor Ai died suddenly; Empress Dowager Wang, mother of Emperor Cheng, summoned her nephew Marquis of Xindu Wang Mang, who seized Dong Xian's seals and ribbons. Xian, afraid, killed himself. Mang then replaced him and also executed the maternal Ding and Fu families.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Ai died suddenly; Empress Dowager Wang, mother of Emperor Cheng, summoned her nephew Marquis of Xindu Wang Mang, who seized Dong Xian's seals and ribbons. Xian, afraid, killed himself. Mang then replaced him and also executed the maternal Ding and Fu families.

sentences.zh: 又廢哀帝傅皇后，令自殺，發掘帝祖母傅太后、母丁太后陵，更以庶人葬之。

translations.literal: He further deposed Empress Fu of Emperor Ai and ordered her suicide, exhumed the tombs of Emperor Ai's grandmother Empress Dowager Fu and mother Empress Dowager Ding, and reburied them as commoners.
translations.idiomatic: He further deposed Empress Fu of Emperor Ai and ordered her suicide, exhumed the tombs of Emperor Ai's grandmother Empress Dowager Fu and mother Empress Dowager Ding, and reburied them as commoners.

sentences.zh: 辜及至尊，大臣微弱之禍也。

translations.literal: When disaster reaches even the highest, it is the calamity of weak great ministers.
translations.idiomatic: When disaster reaches even the highest, it is the calamity of weak great ministers.

sentences.zh: 文公十一年，「敗狄于鹹」。

translations.literal: In Duke Wen year eleven: "They defeated the Di at Xian."
translations.idiomatic: In Duke Wen year eleven: "They defeated the Di at Xian."

sentences.zh: 穀梁、公羊傳曰，長狄兄弟三人，一者之魯，一者之齊，一者之晉。

translations.literal: The Guliang and Gongyang Traditions say there were three Long-Di brothers, one came to Lu, one to Qi, one to Jin.
translations.idiomatic: The Guliang and Gongyang Traditions say there were three Long-Di brothers, one came to Lu, one to Qi, one to Jin.

sentences.zh: 皆殺之，身橫九畝；

translations.literal: All were killed, each body spanning nine mu;
translations.idiomatic: All were killed, each body spanning nine mu;

sentences.zh: 斷其首而載之，眉見於軾。

translations.literal: their heads were cut and carried, with brows visible above the chariot crossbar.
translations.idiomatic: their heads were cut and carried, with brows visible above the chariot crossbar.

sentences.zh: 何以書？

translations.literal: Why record this?
translations.idiomatic: Why record this?

sentences.zh: 記異也。

translations.literal: To record anomaly.
translations.idiomatic: To record anomaly.

sentences.zh: 劉向以為是時周室衰微，三國為大，可責者也。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang held that at this time the Zhou house was weak and those three states were great, thus especially blameworthy.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang held that at this time the Zhou house was weak and those three states were great, thus especially blameworthy.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，不行禮義，大為夷狄之行，將至危亡。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was as if to say: if rites and righteousness are not practiced and conduct turns barbarous, danger and ruin will follow.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if to say: if rites and righteousness are not practiced and conduct turns barbarous, danger and ruin will follow.

sentences.zh: 其後三國皆有篡弒之禍，近下人伐上之痾也。

translations.literal: Afterward all three states suffered disasters of usurpation and regicide, near affliction of inferiors attacking superiors.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward all three states suffered disasters of usurpation and regicide, near affliction of inferiors attacking superiors.

sentences.zh: 劉歆以為人變，屬黃祥。

translations.literal: Liu Xin held it a human transformation belonging to yellow portent.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin held it a human transformation belonging to yellow portent.

sentences.zh: 一曰，屬臝蟲之孽。

translations.literal: Another view: it belongs to prodigies of naked insects.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: it belongs to prodigies of naked insects.

sentences.zh: 一曰，天地之性人為貴，凡人為變，皆屬皇極下人伐上之痾云。

translations.literal: Another view: in the nature of Heaven and Earth, humans are most valued; all human transformations belong under the Huangji category of inferiors attacking superiors.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: in the nature of Heaven and Earth, humans are most valued; all human transformations belong under the Huangji category of inferiors attacking superiors.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「君暴亂，疾有道，厥妖長狄入國。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the ruler is violent and chaotic and hates the Way, its portent is Long-Di entering the state."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the ruler is violent and chaotic and hates the Way, its portent is Long-Di entering the state."

sentences.zh: 又曰：「豐其屋，下獨苦。

translations.literal: It also says: "He enriches his halls while those below suffer alone.
translations.idiomatic: It also says: "He enriches his halls while those below suffer alone.

sentences.zh: 長狄生，世主虜。」

translations.literal: Long-Di are born and worldly rulers are captured."
translations.idiomatic: Long-Di are born and worldly rulers are captured."

sentences.zh: 史記秦始皇帝二十六年，有大人長五丈，足履六尺，皆夷狄服，凡十二人，見于臨洮。

translations.literal: The Records says: in year twenty-six of First Emperor of Qin, there were great men five zhang tall with six-chi footprints, all in barbarian dress, twelve in total, seen at Lintao.
translations.idiomatic: The Records says: in year twenty-six of First Emperor of Qin, there were great men five zhang tall with six-chi footprints, all in barbarian dress, twelve in total, seen at Lintao.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，勿大為夷狄之行，將受其禍。

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not greatly adopt barbarian conduct, or you will suffer its calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: do not greatly adopt barbarian conduct, or you will suffer its calamity.

sentences.zh: 是歲始皇初并六國，反喜以為瑞，銷天下兵器，作金人十二以象之。

translations.literal: That year the First Emperor had just unified the six states, but instead rejoiced and took it as an auspicious sign, melted the realm's weapons, and made twelve bronze men in their likeness.
translations.idiomatic: That year the First Emperor had just unified the six states, but instead rejoiced and took it as an auspicious sign, melted the realm's weapons, and made twelve bronze men in their likeness.

sentences.zh: 遂自賢聖，燔詩書，阬儒士；

translations.literal: He then styled himself sage and worthy, burned poems and books, and buried scholars alive;
translations.idiomatic: He then styled himself sage and worthy, burned poems and books, and buried scholars alive;

sentences.zh: 奢淫暴虐，務欲廣地；

translations.literal: extravagant, licentious, and tyrannical, intent on expanding territory;
translations.idiomatic: extravagant, licentious, and tyrannical, intent on expanding territory;

sentences.zh: 南戍五嶺，北築長城以備胡越，塹山填谷，西起臨洮，東至遼東，徑數千里。

translations.literal: They garrisoned the Five Ridges in the south and built the Great Wall in the north against Hu and Yue, cutting through mountains and filling valleys, from Lintao in the west to Liaodong in the east, for thousands of li.
translations.idiomatic: They garrisoned the Five Ridges in the south and built the Great Wall in the north against Hu and Yue, cutting through mountains and filling valleys, from Lintao in the west to Liaodong in the east, for thousands of li.

sentences.zh: 故大人見於臨洮，明禍亂之起。

translations.literal: Therefore the great men appeared at Lintao, making clear where disaster and chaos would begin.
translations.idiomatic: Therefore the great men appeared at Lintao, making clear where disaster and chaos would begin.

sentences.zh: 後十四年而秦亡，亡自戍卒陳勝發。

translations.literal: Fourteen years later Qin perished, with the collapse beginning in the uprising of the conscript Chen Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: Fourteen years later Qin perished, with the collapse beginning in the uprising of the conscript Chen Sheng.

sentences.zh: 史記魏襄王十三年，魏有女子化為丈夫。

translations.literal: The Records says: in year thirteen of King Xiang of Wei, there was a woman transformed into a man.
translations.idiomatic: The Records says: in year thirteen of King Xiang of Wei, there was a woman transformed into a man.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「女子化為丈夫，茲謂陰昌，賤人為王；

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "A woman transformed into man is called yin flourishing; base people become kings;
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "A woman transformed into man is called yin flourishing; base people become kings;

sentences.zh: 丈夫化為女子，茲謂陰勝，厥咎亡。」

translations.literal: A man transformed into a woman is called yin overcoming, and its blame-sign is extinction."
translations.idiomatic: A man transformed into a woman is called yin overcoming, and its blame-sign is extinction."

sentences.zh: 一曰，男化為女，宮刑濫也；

translations.literal: Another view: man becoming woman means castration penalties are abused;
translations.idiomatic: Another view: man becoming woman means castration penalties are abused;

sentences.zh: 女化為男，婦政行也。

translations.literal: woman becoming man means women's governance prevails.
translations.idiomatic: woman becoming man means women's governance prevails.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平中，豫章有男子化為女子，嫁為人婦，生一子。

translations.literal: During Jianping under Emperor Ai, in Yuzhang there was a man transformed into a woman, married as a wife, and bore one son.
translations.idiomatic: During Jianping under Emperor Ai, in Yuzhang there was a man transformed into a woman, married as a wife, and bore one son.

sentences.zh: 長安陳鳳言此陽變為陰，將亡繼嗣，自相生之象。

translations.literal: Chen Feng of Chang'an said this was yang transforming into yin, meaning heir succession would end, an image of self-generation.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Feng of Chang'an said this was yang transforming into yin, meaning heir succession would end, an image of self-generation.

sentences.zh: 一曰，嫁為人婦生一子，將復一世乃絕。

translations.literal: Another view: marrying as wife and bearing one son means one more generation then extinction.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: marrying as wife and bearing one son means one more generation then extinction.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平四年四月，山陽方與女子田無嗇生子。

translations.literal: In fourth month of Jianping year four under Emperor Ai, in Shan'yang, a woman Fangyu and daughter Tian Wuse gave birth to a son.
translations.idiomatic: In fourth month of Jianping year four under Emperor Ai, in Shan'yang, a woman Fangyu and daughter Tian Wuse gave birth to a son.

sentences.zh: 先未生二月，兒啼腹中，及生，不舉，葬之陌上，三日，人過聞啼聲，母掘收養。

translations.literal: Two months before birth the child cried in the womb; when born it was not raised and was buried by the roadside. After three days passersby heard crying, and the mother dug it up and raised it.
translations.idiomatic: Two months before birth the child cried in the womb; when born it was not raised and was buried by the roadside. After three days passersby heard crying, and the mother dug it up and raised it.

sentences.zh: 平帝元始元年二月，朔方廣牧女子趙春病死，斂棺積六日，出在棺外，自言見夫死父，曰：「年二十七，不當死。」

translations.literal: In second month of Yuanshi year one under Emperor Ping, in Guangmu, Shuofang, a woman Zhao Chun fell ill and died. After six days sealed in coffin, she came out outside the coffin and said she had seen her dead husband and father, saying, "At age twenty-seven, I should not die."
translations.idiomatic: In second month of Yuanshi year one under Emperor Ping, in Guangmu, Shuofang, a woman Zhao Chun fell ill and died. After six days sealed in coffin, she came out outside the coffin and said she had seen her dead husband and father, saying, "At age twenty-seven, I should not die."

sentences.zh: 太守譚以聞。

translations.literal: Governor Tan reported it.
translations.idiomatic: Governor Tan reported it.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「『幹父之蠱，有子，考亡咎』。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: ""Handling father's corruption: there is a son; the deceased father has no blame."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: ""Handling father's corruption: there is a son; the deceased father has no blame."

sentences.zh: 子三年不改父道，思慕不皇，亦重見先人之非，不則為私，厥妖人死復生。」

translations.literal: If for three years the son does not alter the father's way and, in longing thought, is not at ease, he may again see forebears' wrongs; otherwise it turns private. Its portent is the dead returning to life."
translations.idiomatic: If for three years the son does not alter the father's way and, in longing thought, is not at ease, he may again see forebears' wrongs; otherwise it turns private. Its portent is the dead returning to life."

sentences.zh: 一曰，至陰為陽，下人為上。

translations.literal: Another view: utmost yin becoming yang, inferiors becoming superiors.
translations.idiomatic: Another view: utmost yin becoming yang, inferiors becoming superiors.

sentences.zh: 六月，長安女子有生兒，兩頭異頸面相鄉，四臂共匈俱前鄉，犊上有目長二寸所。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, a woman in Chang'an gave birth to a child with two heads on separate necks facing each other, four arms sharing a single chest and both facing forward, and an eye on the lower body about two cun long.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month, a woman in Chang'an gave birth to a child with two heads on separate necks facing each other, four arms sharing a single chest and both facing forward, and an eye on the lower body about two cun long.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「『睽孤，見豕負塗』，厥妖人生兩頭。

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: ""Isolated in opposition, one sees swine covered in mud"; its portent is humans born with two heads.
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: ""Isolated in opposition, one sees swine covered in mud"; its portent is humans born with two heads.

sentences.zh: 下相攘善，妖亦同。

translations.literal: When those below contend over good, the portent is likewise so.
translations.idiomatic: When those below contend over good, the portent is likewise so.

sentences.zh: 人若六畜首目在下，茲謂亡上，正將變更。

translations.literal: If humans or livestock have head and eyes below, this is called no superior, and proper order will change.
translations.idiomatic: If humans or livestock have head and eyes below, this is called no superior, and proper order will change.

sentences.zh: 凡妖之作，以譴失正，各象其類。

translations.literal: Whenever portents arise, they admonish loss of correctness, each resembling its own class.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever portents arise, they admonish loss of correctness, each resembling its own class.

sentences.zh: 二首，下不壹也；

translations.literal: Two heads mean those below are not unified.
translations.idiomatic: Two heads mean those below are not unified.

sentences.zh: 足多，所任邪也；

translations.literal: Many feet mean what is entrusted is deviant.
translations.idiomatic: Many feet mean what is entrusted is deviant.

sentences.zh: 足少，下不勝任，或不任下也。

translations.literal: Few feet mean those below cannot bear responsibility, or those above do not entrust those below.
translations.idiomatic: Few feet mean those below cannot bear responsibility, or those above do not entrust those below.

sentences.zh: 凡下體生於上，不敬也；

translations.literal: Whenever lower body parts grow above, it means irreverence;
translations.idiomatic: Whenever lower body parts grow above, it means irreverence;

sentences.zh: 上體生於下，媟瀆也；

translations.literal: Upper body parts growing below means desecration.
translations.idiomatic: Upper body parts growing below means desecration.

sentences.zh: 生非其類，淫亂也；

translations.literal: Birth outside one's kind means licentious disorder.
translations.idiomatic: Birth outside one's kind means licentious disorder.

sentences.zh: 人生而大，上速成也；

translations.literal: Being born already large means those above mature too quickly.
translations.idiomatic: Being born already large means those above mature too quickly.

sentences.zh: 生而能言，好虛也。

translations.literal: Being born able to speak means a fondness for empty words.
translations.idiomatic: Being born able to speak means a fondness for empty words.

sentences.zh: 群妖推此類，不改乃成凶也。」

translations.literal: By this kind of reasoning for all portents, if there is no reform they become full calamity."
translations.idiomatic: By this kind of reasoning for all portents, if there is no reform they become full calamity."

sentences.zh: 景帝二年九月，膠東下密人年七十餘，生角，角有毛。

translations.literal: In ninth month of Emperor Jing year two, a man over seventy in Xiami, Jiaodong, grew horns with hair on them.
translations.idiomatic: In ninth month of Emperor Jing year two, a man over seventy in Xiami, Jiaodong, grew horns with hair on them.

sentences.zh: 時膠東、膠西、濟南、齊四主有舉兵反謀，謀由吳王濞起，連楚、趙，凡七國。

translations.literal: At that time the four rulers of Jiaodong, Jiaoxi, Jinan, and Qi were plotting armed rebellion; the plot began with King Pi of Wu, linking Chu and Zhao, seven states in all.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the four rulers of Jiaodong, Jiaoxi, Jinan, and Qi were plotting armed rebellion; the plot began with King Pi of Wu, linking Chu and Zhao, seven states in all.

sentences.zh: 下密，縣居四齊之中；

translations.literal: Xiami county lay in the middle of those four Qi states;
translations.idiomatic: Xiami county lay in the middle of those four Qi states;

sentences.zh: 角，兵象，上鄉者也；

translations.literal: horns symbolize arms, pointing upward;
translations.idiomatic: horns symbolize arms, pointing upward;

sentences.zh: 老人，吳王象也；

translations.literal: the old man symbolized King of Wu;
translations.idiomatic: the old man symbolized King of Wu;

sentences.zh: 年七十，七國象也。

translations.literal: age seventy symbolized the seven states.
translations.idiomatic: age seventy symbolized the seven states.

sentences.zh: 天戒若曰，人不當生角，猶諸侯不當舉兵以鄉京師也；

translations.literal: Heaven's warning was as if saying: people should not grow horns, just as feudal lords should not raise troops toward the capital;
translations.idiomatic: Heaven's warning was as if saying: people should not grow horns, just as feudal lords should not raise troops toward the capital;

sentences.zh: 禍從老人生，七國俱敗云。

translations.literal: disaster would arise from an old man, and all seven states would be defeated.
translations.idiomatic: disaster would arise from an old man, and all seven states would be defeated.

sentences.zh: 諸侯不寤，明年吳王先起，諸侯從之，七國俱滅。

translations.literal: The feudal lords did not awaken. Next year the King of Wu rose first, the feudal lords followed, and all seven states were destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: The feudal lords did not awaken. Next year the King of Wu rose first, the feudal lords followed, and all seven states were destroyed.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「冢宰專政，厥妖人生角。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the chief minister monopolizes government, its portent is people growing horns."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When the chief minister monopolizes government, its portent is people growing horns."

sentences.zh: 成帝建始三年十月丁未，京師相驚，言大水至。

translations.literal: On dingwei day of tenth month, Jianshi year three under Emperor Cheng, the capital was alarmed by rumors that great floods were arriving.
translations.idiomatic: On dingwei day of tenth month, Jianshi year three under Emperor Cheng, the capital was alarmed by rumors that great floods were arriving.

sentences.zh: 渭水虒上小女陳持弓年九歲，走入橫城門，入未央宮尚方掖門，殿門門衛戶者莫見，至句盾禁中而覺得。

translations.literal: A nine-year-old girl of Wei-shang at Chen named Chi Gong ran into Hengcheng Gate, entered the side gate of the palace workshops in Weiyang, and none of the hall gate guards saw her. She was only noticed when she reached the internal district of the military guard.
translations.idiomatic: A nine-year-old girl of Wei-shang at Chen named Chi Gong ran into Hengcheng Gate, entered the side gate of the palace workshops in Weiyang, and none of the hall gate guards saw her. She was only noticed when she reached the internal district of the military guard.

sentences.zh: 民以水相驚者，陰氣盛也。

translations.literal: People alarming one another about floods meant yin qi was excessive.
translations.idiomatic: People alarming one another about floods meant yin qi was excessive.

sentences.zh: 小女而入宮殿中者，下人將因女寵而居有宮室之象也。

translations.literal: A little girl entering the palace halls symbolized inferiors using female favor to occupy palace chambers.
translations.idiomatic: A little girl entering the palace halls symbolized inferiors using female favor to occupy palace chambers.

sentences.zh: 名曰持弓，有似周家览弧之祥。

translations.literal: Her name, Chi Gong (holding bow), resembled the Zhou omen of mulberry bow.
translations.idiomatic: Her name, Chi Gong (holding bow), resembled the Zhou omen of mulberry bow.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「弧矢之利，以威天下。」

translations.literal: The Changes says: "The benefit of bow and arrow is to overawe the realm."
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says: "The benefit of bow and arrow is to overawe the realm."

sentences.zh: 是時，帝母王太后弟鳳始為上將，秉國政，天知其後將威天下而入宮室，故象先見也。

translations.literal: At that time Wang Feng, younger brother of the emperor's mother Empress Dowager Wang, had just become supreme general and held state power. Heaven knew he would later dominate the realm and enter palace authority, so the sign appeared first.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Wang Feng, younger brother of the emperor's mother Empress Dowager Wang, had just become supreme general and held state power. Heaven knew he would later dominate the realm and enter palace authority, so the sign appeared first.

sentences.zh: 其後，王氏兄弟父子五侯秉權，至莽卒篡天下，蓋陳氏之後云。

translations.literal: Afterward the Wang brothers and father-son Five Marquises held power, until Mang finally usurped the realm; likely this was the later Chen household omen.
translations.idiomatic: Afterward the Wang brothers and father-son Five Marquises held power, until Mang finally usurped the realm; likely this was the later Chen household omen.

sentences.zh: 京房易傳曰：「妖言動眾，茲謂不信，路將亡人，司馬死。」

translations.literal: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When demagogic words stir the masses, this is called untrustworthiness; roads will lose people, and the marshal will die."
translations.idiomatic: Jing Fang's Yi Tradition says: "When demagogic words stir the masses, this is called untrustworthiness; roads will lose people, and the marshal will die."

sentences.zh: 成帝綏和二年八月庚申，鄭通里男子王褒衣絳衣小冠，帶劍入北司馬門殿東門，上前殿，入非常室中，解帷組結佩之，招前殿署長業等曰：「天帝令我居此。」

translations.literal: On gengshen day of eighth month, Suihe year two under Emperor Cheng, a man of Zhengtongli, Wang Bao, dressed in crimson clothes and small cap with sword at his belt, entered the eastern gate of the palace hall through North Sima Gate, went up to the front hall, entered a restricted chamber, untied curtain cords and tied them as pendants, and beckoned office chiefs such as Ye saying: "The Heavenly Emperor orders me to dwell here."
translations.idiomatic: On gengshen day of eighth month, Suihe year two under Emperor Cheng, a man of Zhengtongli, Wang Bao, dressed in crimson clothes and small cap with sword at his belt, entered the eastern gate of the palace hall through North Sima Gate, went up to the front hall, entered a restricted chamber, untied curtain cords and tied them as pendants, and beckoned office chiefs such as Ye saying: "The Heavenly Emperor orders me to dwell here."

sentences.zh: 業等收縛考問，褒故公車大誰卒，病狂易，不自知入宮狀，下獄死。

translations.literal: Ye and others seized, bound, and interrogated him. Bao was formerly a clerk of the public carriage office, mentally deranged and unaware of how he entered the palace. He was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Ye and others seized, bound, and interrogated him. Bao was formerly a clerk of the public carriage office, mentally deranged and unaware of how he entered the palace. He was imprisoned and died.

sentences.zh: 是時王莽為大司馬，哀帝即位，莽乞骸骨就第，天知其必不退，故因是而見象也。

translations.literal: At that time Wang Mang was Grand Marshal. When Emperor Ai took the throne, Mang requested retirement to his estate. Heaven knew he would not truly withdraw, and therefore showed this sign through the event.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Wang Mang was Grand Marshal. When Emperor Ai took the throne, Mang requested retirement to his estate. Heaven knew he would not truly withdraw, and therefore showed this sign through the event.

sentences.zh: 姓名章服甚明，徑上前殿路寢，入室取組而佩之，稱天帝命，然時人莫察。

translations.literal: His name, attire, and insignia were very clear: he directly entered the front-hall sleeping chamber, took cord and wore it, claiming Heaven's command, yet people then did not perceive it.
translations.idiomatic: His name, attire, and insignia were very clear: he directly entered the front-hall sleeping chamber, took cord and wore it, claiming Heaven's command, yet people then did not perceive it.

sentences.zh: 後莽就國，天下冤之，哀帝徵莽還京師。

translations.literal: Later Mang returned to his fief, and the realm felt wronged for him; Emperor Ai summoned Mang back to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Later Mang returned to his fief, and the realm felt wronged for him; Emperor Ai summoned Mang back to the capital.

sentences.zh: 明年帝崩，莽復為大司馬，因是而篡國。

translations.literal: The next year the emperor died, Mang again became Grand Marshal, and by this he usurped the state.
translations.idiomatic: The next year the emperor died, Mang again became Grand Marshal, and by this he usurped the state.

sentences.zh: 哀帝建平四年正月，民驚走，持稿或棷一枚，傳相付與，曰行詔籌。

translations.literal: In first month of Jianping year four under Emperor Ai, people panicked and ran, each carrying a stalk or a branch, passing them to one another as "edict tally sticks."
translations.idiomatic: In first month of Jianping year four under Emperor Ai, people panicked and ran, each carrying a stalk or a branch, passing them to one another as "edict tally sticks."

sentences.zh: 道中相過逢多至千數，或被髮徒踐，或夜折關，或踰牆入，或乘車騎奔馳，以置驛傳行，經歷郡國二十六，至京師。

translations.literal: On roads those encountering each other reached into the thousands: some with hair loose and barefoot, some breaking gates by night, some climbing walls, some riding carts and horses at speed, using relay posts to spread it, passing through twenty-six commanderies and kingdoms to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: On roads those encountering each other reached into the thousands: some with hair loose and barefoot, some breaking gates by night, some climbing walls, some riding carts and horses at speed, using relay posts to spread it, passing through twenty-six commanderies and kingdoms to the capital.

sentences.zh: 其夏，京師郡國民聚會里巷仟佰，設祭張博具，歌舞祠西王母，又傳書曰：「母告百姓，佩此書者不死。

translations.literal: That summer, in capital and commanderies people gathered in lanes and wards, set sacrifices and gaming equipment, singing and dancing to worship the Queen Mother of the West. Texts were also circulated saying: "The Mother tells the people: those wearing this text will not die.
translations.idiomatic: That summer, in capital and commanderies people gathered in lanes and wards, set sacrifices and gaming equipment, singing and dancing to worship the Queen Mother of the West. Texts were also circulated saying: "The Mother tells the people: those wearing this text will not die.

sentences.zh: 不信我言，視門樞下，當有白髮。」

translations.literal: If you do not believe my words, look beneath your gate pivot; there will be white hair."
translations.idiomatic: If you do not believe my words, look beneath your gate pivot; there will be white hair."

sentences.zh: 至秋止。

translations.literal: It stopped by autumn.
translations.idiomatic: It stopped by autumn.

sentences.zh: 是時帝祖母傅太后驕，與政事，故杜鄴對曰：「春秋災異，以指象為言語。

translations.literal: At that time the emperor's grandmother Empress Dowager Fu was arrogant and interfered in government, so Du Ye replied: "Spring and Autumn disasters and anomalies speak through symbolic indication.
translations.idiomatic: At that time the emperor's grandmother Empress Dowager Fu was arrogant and interfered in government, so Du Ye replied: "Spring and Autumn disasters and anomalies speak through symbolic indication.

sentences.zh: 籌，所以紀數。

translations.literal: Tally sticks are for recording numbers.
translations.idiomatic: Tally sticks are for recording numbers.

sentences.zh: 民，陰，水類也。

translations.literal: The people are yin, of water kind.
translations.idiomatic: The people are yin, of water kind.

sentences.zh: 水以東流為順走，而西行，反類逆上。

translations.literal: Water should flow east as its proper course, but here it moved westward, an inverted and contrary ascent.
translations.idiomatic: Water should flow east as its proper course, but here it moved westward, an inverted and contrary ascent.

sentences.zh: 象數度放溢，妄以相予，違忤民心之應也。

translations.literal: It symbolizes norms and measures spilling out, recklessly passed from person to person, response to violating public sentiment.
translations.idiomatic: It symbolizes norms and measures spilling out, recklessly passed from person to person, response to violating public sentiment.

sentences.zh: 西王母，婦人之稱。

translations.literal: Queen Mother of the West is a title of a woman.
translations.idiomatic: Queen Mother of the West is a title of a woman.

sentences.zh: 博弈，男子之事。

translations.literal: Gaming and gambling are men's affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Gaming and gambling are men's affairs.

sentences.zh: 於街巷仟伯，明離闑內，與疆外。

translations.literal: In lanes and wards it clearly moved outside gate-boundary, beyond proper bounds.
translations.idiomatic: In lanes and wards it clearly moved outside gate-boundary, beyond proper bounds.

sentences.zh: 臨事盤樂，炕陽之意。

translations.literal: Taking pleasure and revelry in the face of affairs shows overbearing yang intent.
translations.idiomatic: Taking pleasure and revelry in the face of affairs shows overbearing yang intent.

sentences.zh: 白髮，衰年之象，體尊性弱，難理易亂。

translations.literal: White hair symbolizes old age: high in status but weak in constitution, difficult to govern and easily thrown into disorder.
translations.idiomatic: White hair symbolizes old age: high in status but weak in constitution, difficult to govern and easily thrown into disorder.

sentences.zh: 門，人之所由；

translations.literal: The gate is where people pass;
translations.idiomatic: The gate is where people pass;

sentences.zh: 樞，其要也。

translations.literal: the pivot is its key point.
translations.idiomatic: the pivot is its key point.

sentences.zh: 居人之所由，制持其要也。

translations.literal: To occupy where people pass is to control and hold their key point.
translations.idiomatic: To occupy where people pass is to control and hold their key point.

sentences.zh: 其明甚者。

translations.literal: Its indication is extremely clear.
translations.idiomatic: Its indication is extremely clear.

sentences.zh: 今外家丁、傅並侍帷幄，布於列位，有罪惡者不坐辜罰，亡功能者畢受官爵。

translations.literal: Now the maternal clans Ding and Fu both serve behind the curtains and fill official ranks; those with crimes and evils receive no punishment, while those without merit receive offices and titles.
translations.idiomatic: Now the maternal clans Ding and Fu both serve behind the curtains and fill official ranks; those with crimes and evils receive no punishment, while those without merit receive offices and titles.

sentences.zh: 皇甫、三桓，詩人所刺，春秋所譏，亡以甚此。

translations.literal: Huangfu and the Three Huan were what poets satirized and Spring and Autumn censured; nothing is more extreme than this.
translations.idiomatic: Huangfu and the Three Huan were what poets satirized and Spring and Autumn censured; nothing is more extreme than this.

sentences.zh: 指象昭昭，以覺聖朝，柰何不應！」

translations.literal: The symbolic signs are perfectly clear to awaken the sacred court. How can one not respond?"
translations.idiomatic: The symbolic signs are perfectly clear to awaken the sacred court. How can one not respond?"

sentences.zh: 後哀帝崩，成帝母王太后臨朝，王莽為大司馬，誅滅丁、傅。

translations.literal: After Emperor Ai died, Empress Dowager Wang, mother of Emperor Cheng, ruled at court; Wang Mang as Grand Marshal executed and exterminated Ding and Fu.
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor Ai died, Empress Dowager Wang, mother of Emperor Cheng, ruled at court; Wang Mang as Grand Marshal executed and exterminated Ding and Fu.

sentences.zh: 一曰丁、傅所亂者小，此異乃王太后、莽之應云。

translations.literal: Another view says the disorder caused by Ding and Fu was minor; this anomaly instead corresponded to Empress Dowager Wang and Mang.
translations.idiomatic: Another view says the disorder caused by Ding and Fu was minor; this anomaly instead corresponded to Empress Dowager Wang and Mang.
